questions.stem: In a taxable business combination, Company Q acquires an identifiable intangible asset with a fair value of $900,000. The tax basis of the asset steps up to $900,000. Under ASC 740, what is the deferred tax effect related to this acquired intangible?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No deferred tax is recognized because book and tax bases are equal

choices.label: D
choices.text: DTL equal to the tax rate times the original book value of the asset

questions.explanation: In a taxable business combination, the acquired assets receive a stepped-up tax basis equal to their fair value. Since the book basis (fair value, $900,000) equals the tax basis ($900,000), there is no temporary difference at the acquisition date. Under ASC 740-10-25-20, deferred taxes arise only when there is a difference between book and tax bases. With no difference, no DTA or DTL is recognized for this asset.
questions.explanationJa: 課税取得の企業結合では、取得資産の税務基準額が公正価値にステップアップする。帳簿基準額（公正価値$900,000）と税務基準額（$900,000）が等しいため、一時差異はなく、繰延税金は認識しない。

questions.stem: Company R extinguishes debt with a carrying amount of $1,200,000 by paying $1,080,000 in cash. For book purposes, a $120,000 gain on extinguishment is recognized. For tax purposes, the gain is also recognized in the current period. What is the deferred tax effect of this transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No deferred tax effect; the gain is a permanent item recognized in both book and tax

choices.label: B
choices.text: DTA of $25,200

choices.label: C
choices.text: DTL of $25,200

questions.explanation: When a gain on debt extinguishment is recognized in the same period for both book and tax purposes, there is no temporary difference. The $120,000 gain increases both book income and taxable income in the same year. Under ASC 740-10-25-20, deferred taxes arise only from temporary differences (timing differences between book and tax recognition). Since both recognize the gain currently, the tax effect is reflected in current tax expense, not deferred taxes.
questions.explanationJa: 債務消滅益$120,000は帳簿上も税務上も同一期間に認識されるため、一時差異は生じない。同一年度に両方で認識される場合、税効果は当期税金費用に反映され、繰延税金は発生しない。

questions.stem: Company S operates in two different tax jurisdictions and has the following deferred tax balances: Jurisdiction A: DTA $120,000 and DTL $80,000; Jurisdiction B: DTA $50,000 and DTL $90,000. How should these be presented on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net noncurrent DTA of $40,000 (Jurisdiction A) and net noncurrent DTL of $40,000 (Jurisdiction B)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net noncurrent DTA of $170,000 and net noncurrent DTL of $170,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Single net noncurrent DTA of $0

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net noncurrent DTA of $40,000 only; the DTL is offset against the DTA

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-45-6, deferred tax assets and liabilities are netted only within the same tax jurisdiction. Jurisdiction A: DTA $120,000 − DTL $80,000 = net DTA $40,000. Jurisdiction B: DTA $50,000 − DTL $90,000 = net DTL $40,000. These two jurisdictions' net amounts are presented separately on the balance sheet as noncurrent items per ASU 2015-17. Cross-jurisdiction netting is prohibited.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-45-6により、DTAとDTLは同一課税管轄区域内でのみ相殺する。管轄A: DTA $120,000 − DTL $80,000 = 純DTA $40,000。管轄B: DTA $50,000 − DTL $90,000 = 純DTL $40,000。異なる管轄区域間の相殺は禁止。それぞれ非流動項目として表示。

questions.stem: Company T has an unrecognized tax benefit of $250,000 related to a tax position taken in Year 1. The statute of limitations for the relevant tax return is 3 years. It is now Year 5, and the statute has expired without examination. What is the appropriate accounting treatment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a $250,000 tax benefit in Year 5 by reversing the liability

choices.label: B
choices.text: Continue to carry the $250,000 liability indefinitely

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reclassify the $250,000 to a contingent liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: Transfer the $250,000 to additional paid-in capital

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-16, when the statute of limitations expires for a tax year without examination by the taxing authority, the uncertain tax position is considered effectively settled. The entity should derecognize the unrecognized tax benefit liability of $250,000, resulting in a $250,000 tax benefit recognized in income tax expense in Year 5. The expiration of the statute means the taxing authority can no longer challenge the position.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-16により、課税当局の調査なく時効が成立した場合、不確実な税務ポジションは事実上解決されたとみなす。未認識税務上の便益$250,000の負債を取り消し、Year 5の法人税等調整額として$250,000の税務上の便益を認識する。

questions.stem: Company U operates in a foreign jurisdiction that grants a 5-year tax holiday, reducing the tax rate from 25% to 0%. Under ASC 740, what disclosures are required related to the tax holiday?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The aggregate dollar and per-share effects of the tax holiday on continuing operations

choices.label: B
choices.text: No specific disclosures are required for tax holidays

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the duration of the tax holiday must be disclosed

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only the name of the foreign jurisdiction must be disclosed

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-50-15A, an entity that benefits from a tax holiday must disclose (1) the nature and terms of the tax holiday, (2) the aggregate dollar effect on income tax expense, and (3) the per-share effect on diluted EPS. These disclosures help users understand the impact of favorable tax terms that may expire and affect future tax rates.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-50-15Aにより、税制優遇措置（タックスホリデー）を受ける企業は、(1)税制優遇の性質と条件、(2)法人税費用への総額影響、(3)希薄化後EPSへの1株当たり影響を開示する必要がある。

questions.subtopic: Intra-Entity Transfer - Inventory to Fixed Asset
questions.stem: Parent Company V sells inventory (cost $300,000) to its subsidiary for $450,000. The subsidiary capitalizes the inventory as a fixed asset and depreciates it over 10 years. For tax purposes, the intercompany profit was taxed at the seller's 21% rate. Under ASU 2016-16, what is the deferred tax effect at the date of the intercompany transfer?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a DTA of $31,500 for the intercompany profit

choices.label: B
choices.text: No deferred tax effect; defer the tax until the asset is sold to an outside party

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a DTL of $31,500

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a DTA of $94,500

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-16 (ASC 740-10-25-3(e)), the income tax consequences of intra-entity transfers of assets other than inventory are recognized when the transfer occurs (rather than deferred). The intercompany profit is $450,000 − $300,000 = $150,000. This profit was taxed at 21%, creating a tax payment of $150,000 × 21% = $31,500. For consolidated book purposes, the profit is eliminated and the asset's basis is $300,000, but the tax basis is $450,000. The difference creates a DTA of $150,000 × 21% = $31,500. Note: ASU 2016-16 applies to non-inventory assets; the subsidiary capitalizes this as a fixed asset.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-16により、棚卸資産以外のグループ内資産移転の税効果は移転時に認識する。内部利益 = $450,000 − $300,000 = $150,000。税金支払 = $150,000 × 21% = $31,500。連結上は資産簿価$300,000だが税務基準額は$450,000。DTA = $150,000 × 21% = $31,500。子会社は固定資産として資産計上するため、ASU 2016-16の対象。

questions.stem: Under ASC 740, how should an entity account for the deferred tax effects of its investment in a partnership (where it is not the tax owner of the underlying assets)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record deferred taxes based on the outside basis difference between the book and tax basis of the partnership investment

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record deferred taxes based on the inside basis differences of the partnership's individual assets and liabilities

choices.label: C
choices.text: No deferred tax is required because partnerships are pass-through entities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record deferred taxes only when the investor expects to sell the partnership interest

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-3, when an entity is not the tax owner of the underlying assets and liabilities (i.e., the partnership is the tax owner), the investor recognizes deferred taxes based on the outside basis difference — the difference between the financial reporting carrying amount of the investment and its tax basis. The inside basis approach (B) applies only when the entity is the tax owner. Deferred taxes are required regardless of whether a sale is expected (D), and pass-through status (C) does not eliminate the need for deferred taxes at the investor level.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-3により、投資先の資産・負債の税務上の所有者でない場合、投資の帳簿価額と税務基準額の差額（outside basis difference）に基づいて繰延税金を認識する。inside basis approach(B)は税務上の所有者の場合のみ。パススルー・エンティティでも投資家レベルでの繰延税金は必要(C)。

questions.subtopic: Interim Discrete Items vs Annual Rate
questions.stem: In the second quarter, Company W settles a lawsuit and pays a $500,000 nondeductible fine. How should this item be treated in the interim tax provision?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a discrete item recognized entirely in the second quarter

choices.label: B
choices.text: Spread proportionally over the remaining quarters using the estimated annual effective tax rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an adjustment to the estimated annual effective tax rate for all quarters

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred until the annual financial statements are prepared

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-270-25-5, unusual or infrequently occurring items that are separately determinable should be treated as discrete items in the interim period in which they occur, rather than being included in the estimated annual effective tax rate calculation. A nondeductible fine from a lawsuit settlement qualifies as such a discrete item. The tax effect (in this case, no deduction = permanent difference) is recognized entirely in Q2.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-270-25-5により、異常または稀にしか発生しない項目で個別に決定可能なものは、見積年間実効税率の計算に含めず、発生した四半期に離散項目（discrete item）として処理する。控除不能な罰金はこのような離散項目に該当し、Q2で全額認識する。

questions.stem: Company X accrues $800,000 in restructuring charges (employee severance) for book purposes. For tax purposes, severance is deductible only when paid. If $200,000 is paid in the current year and the remainder will be paid next year, what is the deferred tax asset at year-end at a 21% tax rate?
questions.explanation: Total restructuring accrual = $800,000. Amount paid in current year = $200,000 (deducted for both book and tax). Remaining accrual at year-end = $800,000 − $200,000 = $600,000. This remaining liability has a book basis of $600,000 and a tax basis of $0 (not yet deductible for tax). The temporary difference is $600,000, creating a future deductible amount. DTA = $600,000 × 21% = $126,000.
questions.explanationJa: リストラ引当金合計$800,000のうち、当期支払$200,000（帳簿・税務とも費用処理済み）。期末残高 = $600,000。帳簿上の負債$600,000に対し税務基準額$0。将来減算一時差異$600,000。DTA = $600,000 × 21% = $126,000。

questions.subtopic: Current vs Deferred Tax Journal Entry
questions.stem: Company Y has pre-tax book income of $1,000,000, a temporary difference of $200,000 (future taxable), and a permanent difference of $50,000 (nondeductible expense). The tax rate is 21%. What is the correct journal entry for total income tax expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Dr. Income Tax Expense $220,500; Cr. Current Tax Payable $178,500; Cr. Deferred Tax Liability $42,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Dr. Income Tax Expense $210,000; Cr. Current Tax Payable $168,000; Cr. Deferred Tax Liability $42,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Dr. Income Tax Expense $262,500; Cr. Current Tax Payable $262,500

choices.label: D
choices.text: Dr. Income Tax Expense $252,000; Cr. Current Tax Payable $210,000; Cr. Deferred Tax Liability $42,000

questions.explanation: Taxable income = Book income $1,000,000 − temporary difference $200,000 (book > tax, so taxable income is less currently) + permanent difference $50,000 (nondeductible, added back) = $850,000. Current tax payable = $850,000 × 21% = $178,500. Deferred tax liability = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000. Total income tax expense = $178,500 + $42,000 = $220,500. Journal entry: Dr. Income Tax Expense $220,500; Cr. Current Tax Payable $178,500; Cr. DTL $42,000.

questions.stem: Company Z holds available-for-sale (AFS) debt securities with an amortized cost of $600,000 and a fair value of $520,000 at year-end. The unrealized loss of $80,000 is reported in OCI. For tax purposes, the loss is not recognized until the securities are sold. At a 21% tax rate, what deferred tax asset is recorded and where is the offset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: DTA of $16,800 with the offset in OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: DTA of $16,800 with the offset in income tax expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: DTL of $16,800 with the offset in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: DTA of $80,000 with the offset in OCI

questions.explanation: The unrealized loss of $80,000 reduces the book carrying amount to $520,000, but the tax basis remains at $600,000. The tax basis exceeds the book value by $80,000, creating a future deductible temporary difference. DTA = $80,000 × 21% = $16,800. Under ASC 740-20-45-2 (intraperiod tax allocation), because the unrealized loss is reported in OCI, the related deferred tax effect must also be allocated to OCI, not income tax expense.
questions.explanationJa: 未実現損失$80,000により帳簿価額は$520,000に減少するが、税務基準額は$600,000のまま。税務基準額が帳簿価額を$80,000上回り、将来減算一時差異。DTA = $80,000 × 21% = $16,800。未実現損失がOCIに計上されているため、ASC 740-20-45-2により繰延税金効果もOCIに配分する。

questions.subtopic: Tax Credits - Foreign Tax Credit Limitation
questions.stem: Company AA has worldwide pre-tax income of $5,000,000, of which $1,000,000 is foreign-source income. The U.S. tax rate is 21%. Foreign taxes paid are $280,000. What is the foreign tax credit limitation?
questions.explanation: The foreign tax credit limitation = U.S. tax × (Foreign-source income / Worldwide income) = ($5,000,000 × 21%) × ($1,000,000 / $5,000,000) = $1,050,000 × 20% = $210,000. The company paid $280,000 in foreign taxes, but can only credit $210,000 in the current year. The excess $70,000 can be carried forward. Under ASC 740-10-25-39, the limitation ensures the FTC does not exceed the U.S. tax attributable to foreign-source income.

questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 2, Company BB had a net DTL of $84,000. During Year 2, the existing taxable temporary difference increased by $120,000, and a new deductible temporary difference of $60,000 arose. The tax rate remained at 21%. What is the ending net DTL?
questions.explanation: Beginning net DTL = $84,000. The taxable temporary difference increased by $120,000, adding $120,000 × 21% = $25,200 to the DTL. A new deductible temporary difference of $60,000 creates a DTA of $60,000 × 21% = $12,600. Ending net DTL = $84,000 + $25,200 − $12,600 = $96,600. Under ASC 740-10-25-20, deferred taxes are adjusted each period to reflect changes in temporary differences at the enacted rate.

questions.stem: Company CC has pre-tax book income of $2,000,000. Included in this income are $100,000 of entertainment expenses (100% nondeductible under TCJA) and $40,000 of fines and penalties (100% nondeductible). Assuming no temporary differences and a 21% tax rate, what is the total income tax expense?
questions.explanation: Under TCJA, entertainment expenses are 100% nondeductible, and fines/penalties are also 100% nondeductible. These are permanent differences that increase taxable income above book income. Permanent add-backs: entertainment $100,000 + fines $40,000 = $140,000. Taxable income = $2,000,000 + $140,000 = $2,140,000. With only permanent differences (no temporary differences), there is no deferred tax effect. Total income tax expense = current tax provision = $2,140,000 × 21% = $449,400.

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax - Step-Up in Tax Basis
questions.stem: In a taxable acquisition, Company DD acquires a building with a book fair value of $2,000,000 and a stepped-up tax basis of $2,000,000. Two years later, book carrying amount is $1,800,000 (straight-line depreciation over 20 years) and tax basis is $1,600,000 (accelerated depreciation). What is the deferred tax liability at that date at a 21% tax rate?
questions.explanation: Book carrying amount after 2 years = $1,800,000. Tax basis after 2 years = $1,600,000. Temporary difference = $1,800,000 − $1,600,000 = $200,000. Book value exceeds tax basis, creating a future taxable temporary difference (future book depreciation will exceed future tax depreciation, resulting in higher taxable income). DTL = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000.

questions.stem: Parent Company EE files a consolidated tax return with its wholly owned subsidiary. Under a tax sharing agreement, the subsidiary pays the parent for the tax benefit of losses used by the consolidated group. The subsidiary has a $300,000 NOL that offsets the parent's taxable income. At a 21% rate, how does the subsidiary account for this under the separate-return method?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a $63,000 receivable from the parent and a $63,000 tax benefit

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize a $63,000 DTA and a valuation allowance

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize no benefit because the subsidiary has no taxable income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a $300,000 receivable from the parent

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-27, when a tax sharing agreement provides for payment of tax benefits, the subsidiary recognizes the benefit based on the agreement terms. The subsidiary's NOL of $300,000 × 21% = $63,000 benefit. Under the separate-return method, the subsidiary accounts for taxes as if it filed a separate return, but the tax sharing agreement creates an intercompany receivable for the amount the parent owes. The subsidiary records: Dr. Receivable from Parent $63,000; Cr. Income Tax Benefit $63,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-27により、税負担配分契約で税務上の便益の支払が規定されている場合、子会社は契約条件に基づいて便益を認識する。子会社のNOL $300,000 × 21% = $63,000の便益。仕訳: (借方)親会社への未収金$63,000 / (貸方)法人税等調整額$63,000。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Expense vs Current Tax Expense Components
questions.stem: Foster Corp. began Year 1 with no deferred tax balances. At December 31, Year 1, it has a cumulative taxable temporary difference of $300,000 from depreciation. Taxable income for Year 1 is $700,000. The enacted tax rate is 21%. What amounts should Foster report as current income tax expense and deferred income tax expense, respectively, for Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Current $147,000; Deferred $63,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Current $210,000; Deferred $63,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Current $147,000; Deferred $0

choices.label: D
choices.text: Current $63,000; Deferred $147,000

questions.explanation: Current income tax expense equals taxable income times the enacted rate: $700,000 × 21% = $147,000 (this also equals current tax payable). Deferred income tax expense equals the change in the deferred tax liability during the year. Beginning DTL = $0; ending DTL = $300,000 × 21% = $63,000. Deferred tax expense = $63,000 − $0 = $63,000. Therefore current expense is $147,000 and deferred expense is $63,000.
questions.explanationJa: 当期法人税費用は課税所得×制定税率：$700,000 × 21% = $147,000（当期未払税金と同額）。繰延法人税費用はDTLの増減：期首DTL $0、期末DTL = $300,000 × 21% = $63,000。繰延税金費用 = $63,000 − $0 = $63,000。よって当期分$147,000、繰延分$63,000。

questions.subtopic: Prepaid Expense Deducted Currently for Tax
questions.stem: Hartwell Inc. prepays $120,000 of insurance on December 31, Year 1, covering the following 12 months. For book purposes the entire $120,000 is recorded as a prepaid asset and expensed over Year 2. For tax purposes the $120,000 is fully deductible in Year 1 under the 12-month rule. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What deferred tax item arises at December 31, Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $30,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $30,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $120,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No deferred tax because prepaids are permanent differences

questions.explanation: For book purposes the prepaid insurance asset has a carrying amount of $120,000. For tax purposes the amount was already deducted, so the tax basis of the asset is $0. The book basis exceeds the tax basis by $120,000, a taxable temporary difference (in Year 2 the book expense will reduce book income but no further tax deduction is available, producing higher taxable income). DTL = $120,000 × 25% = $30,000.
questions.explanationJa: 帳簿上の前払保険料の簿価は$120,000。税務上は既に控除済みのため税務基準額$0。帳簿価額が税務基準額を$120,000上回る将来加算一時差異（Year 2に帳簿費用は計上するが税務控除はないため課税所得が増える）。DTL = $120,000 × 25% = $30,000。

questions.stem: Madison Co. sells property in Year 1 and recognizes the entire $500,000 gain in book income immediately. For tax purposes, Madison elects the installment method and will report the gain ratably over five years ($100,000 per year, Years 1 through 5). The enacted tax rate is 21%. After recording Year 1, what is the deferred tax liability related to the installment receivable?
questions.explanation: Book recognizes the full $500,000 gain in Year 1. Tax recognizes $100,000 in Year 1, leaving $400,000 of gain to be taxed in Years 2 through 5. The $400,000 of gain recognized for book but not yet for tax is a taxable temporary difference (future taxable amount). DTL = $400,000 × 21% = $84,000.
questions.explanationJa: 帳簿はYear 1に利益$500,000全額を認識。税務はYear 1に$100,000のみ認識し、$400,000はYears 2〜5に課税される。帳簿上認識済みだが税務未認識の$400,000が将来加算一時差異。DTL = $400,000 × 21% = $84,000。

questions.stem: In a nontaxable business combination, Acquirer Corp. acquires equipment with a fair value of $700,000, but the carryover tax basis is only $500,000. The enacted tax rate is 25%. How is the resulting deferred tax recognized, and how does it affect the bargain purchase or goodwill computation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record a deferred tax asset of $50,000, which reduces goodwill

choices.label: B
choices.text: No deferred tax because fair value adjustments in a combination are exempt

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record a deferred tax liability of $175,000 that is charged to income tax expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record a deferred tax liability of $50,000, which increases goodwill (or reduces a bargain purchase gain)

questions.explanation: In a nontaxable (carryover-basis) combination, deferred taxes are recognized for differences between assigned fair value and carryover tax basis under ASC 805-740-25-2. Book fair value $700,000 exceeds tax basis $500,000 by $200,000, a taxable temporary difference. DTL = $200,000 × 25% = $50,000. The DTL is part of the net identifiable assets/liabilities assumed; recording an additional liability reduces net identifiable assets, which increases goodwill (or decreases any bargain purchase gain).
questions.explanationJa: 非課税（簿価引継）の企業結合では、付与公正価値と引継税務基準額の差額について繰延税金を認識する（ASC 805-740-25-2）。公正価値$700,000が税務基準額$500,000を$200,000上回る将来加算一時差異。DTL = $200,000 × 25% = $50,000。このDTLは取得純資産の一部であり、負債計上により純識別可能資産が減少し、のれんが増加（または割安購入益が減少）する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 740-10-50, which of the following must a public business entity disclose regarding its deferred tax accounts?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The total of all deferred tax liabilities, the total of all deferred tax assets, and the total valuation allowance, with the net change in the valuation allowance during the year

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the net deferred tax liability or asset, with no breakdown by component

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only deferred tax assets; deferred tax liabilities need not be disclosed

choices.label: D
choices.text: The fair value of each deferred tax asset and liability

questions.explanation: ASC 740-10-50-2 requires disclosure of the total of all deferred tax liabilities, the total of all deferred tax assets, and the total valuation allowance recognized for deferred tax assets, along with the net change in the total valuation allowance during the year. A net-only presentation, omitting deferred tax liabilities, or fair value measurement are not the required disclosures; deferred taxes are measured at undiscounted enacted rates, not fair value.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-50-2は、全繰延税金負債合計、全繰延税金資産合計、評価性引当金合計、および当期の評価性引当金純増減の開示を要求する。純額のみの表示、繰延税金負債の省略、公正価値測定はいずれも誤り。繰延税金は割引前の制定税率で測定し、公正価値では測定しない。

questions.stem: Norwood Corp. makes a $500,000 charitable contribution in Year 1. Tax law limits the current-year deduction to 10% of taxable income, which is $300,000; the excess $200,000 is carried forward up to five years. Book expense is the full $500,000. The enacted tax rate is 21%. Assuming realization is more likely than not, what deferred tax asset arises from the contribution carryforward?
questions.explanation: Book deducts the full $500,000 contribution in Year 1, but tax allows only $300,000 currently. The disallowed $200,000 becomes a charitable contribution carryforward, deductible in future years (a future deductible amount). DTA = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000. Because realization is more likely than not, no valuation allowance is required.
questions.explanationJa: 帳簿はYear 1に寄附金$500,000全額を費用計上するが、税務は当期$300,000のみ控除可能。控除不能の$200,000は繰越控除（将来減算一時差異）。DTA = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000。実現がmore likely than notのため評価性引当金は不要。

questions.stem: Pinnacle Corp. owns 15% of a domestic corporation and receives $200,000 in dividends, qualifying for a 50% dividends received deduction (DRD). Pinnacle's pretax book income, which includes the full $200,000 dividend, is $1,000,000. There are no other differences. The tax rate is 21%. What is Pinnacle's current income tax expense?
questions.explanation: The dividends received deduction is a permanent difference that reduces taxable income but not book income. DRD = $200,000 × 50% = $100,000. Taxable income = $1,000,000 − $100,000 = $900,000. Current income tax expense = $900,000 × 21% = $189,000. Because the DRD is permanent (it never reverses), there is no deferred tax effect; the full tax effect is current.
questions.explanationJa: 受取配当控除（DRD）は課税所得を減らすが帳簿利益には影響しない永久差異。DRD = $200,000 × 50% = $100,000。課税所得 = $1,000,000 − $100,000 = $900,000。当期法人税費用 = $900,000 × 21% = $189,000。DRDは永久差異のため繰延税効果はなく、全額当期。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax on Bond Discount Amortization
questions.stem: Riverside Corp. issued bonds at a discount. For book purposes it uses the effective-interest method; for tax purposes the original issue discount (OID) is amortized on a similar but not identical schedule. At year-end, the book carrying amount of the bond liability is $960,000 and the tax basis of the liability is $945,000. The enacted tax rate is 21%. What deferred tax item arises?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $3,150

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $15,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No deferred tax because both use OID amortization

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $3,150

questions.explanation: For a liability, a deductible temporary difference (DTA) arises when the book carrying amount exceeds the tax basis. Book liability $960,000 exceeds tax basis $945,000 by $15,000. When the liability is settled, the lower tax basis means future tax deductions (or smaller taxable amounts) — effectively a future deductible amount. DTA = $15,000 × 21% = $3,150.
questions.explanationJa: 負債では、帳簿価額が税務基準額を上回ると将来減算一時差異（DTA）が生じる。帳簿負債$960,000が税務基準額$945,000を$15,000上回る。負債決済時に税務基準額が低いことで将来の税務控除が生じる。DTA = $15,000 × 21% = $3,150。

questions.subtopic: Valuation Allowance Establishment Charged to Expense
questions.stem: At the end of Year 1, Sterling Corp. has a gross deferred tax asset of $400,000 and no valuation allowance. Continued operating losses lead management to conclude at the end of Year 2 that it is more likely than not that $250,000 of the now $400,000 DTA will not be realized. The DTA balance is unchanged at $400,000. What is the Year 2 income statement effect of establishing the valuation allowance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase income tax expense by $250,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase income tax expense by $400,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease income tax expense by $250,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No income statement effect; charge directly to equity

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-5, a valuation allowance is established when it becomes more likely than not that some portion of the DTA will not be realized. The required allowance is $250,000. The entry is: Dr. Income Tax Expense $250,000; Cr. Valuation Allowance $250,000. Establishing or increasing a valuation allowance that relates to deductible differences originating in continuing operations is recognized as additional income tax expense, increasing expense by $250,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-5により、DTAの一部が実現しない可能性がmore likely than notになった時点で評価性引当金を設定する。必要引当額$250,000。仕訳: (借方)法人税費用$250,000 / (貸方)評価性引当金$250,000。継続事業に係る評価性引当金の設定・増加は追加の法人税費用として認識され、費用が$250,000増加する。

questions.subtopic: Total Tax Expense with Loss Carryforward Utilization
questions.stem: Calloway Corp. begins Year 2 with a $200,000 NOL carryforward (and a corresponding $42,000 DTA, no valuation allowance) generated post-2017. In Year 2, taxable income before the NOL deduction is $500,000. The 80% limitation applies. The tax rate is 21%. What is Calloway's total income tax expense for Year 2?
questions.explanation: The 80% limitation caps the NOL deduction at $500,000 × 80% = $400,000, which exceeds the available $200,000 carryforward, so the full $200,000 NOL is used. Taxable income after NOL = $500,000 − $200,000 = $300,000. Current tax = $300,000 × 21% = $63,000. Using the NOL reverses the $42,000 DTA, which is deferred tax expense of $42,000. Total income tax expense = current $63,000 + deferred $42,000 = $105,000. (Equivalently, $500,000 × 21% = $105,000 since no permanent differences exist.)

questions.subtopic: Mid-Year Rate Change Blended Annual Rate
questions.stem: A tax law enacted on July 1, Year 1 changes the corporate rate from 30% to 20%, effective for the entire calendar Year 1 (a blended rate does not apply; the new rate applies to all of Year 1 income). Tillman Corp. had recorded a Year 1 first-half current tax provision of $90,000 on $300,000 of year-to-date taxable income at 30%. Year 1 total taxable income is $600,000. What total current tax provision should Tillman report for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Because the enacted rate of 20% applies to all of Year 1 income, the current tax for the full year is computed at the new rate: $600,000 × 20% = $120,000. The earlier $90,000 provision recorded at 30% is corrected within the same annual period to reflect the enacted rate. There is no blended computation here; the full-year liability is simply $120,000.
questions.explanationJa: 制定税率20%がYear 1全体の所得に適用されるため、通年の当期税金は新税率で計算：$600,000 × 20% = $120,000。30%で計上した前半の$90,000は同一年度内で制定税率を反映するよう修正する。混合計算は不要で、通年の税負担は$120,000。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax on Unremitted Foreign Earnings Disclosure
questions.stem: Under ASC 740-30-50, if an entity does NOT recognize a deferred tax liability on the undistributed earnings of a foreign subsidiary because of the indefinite reinvestment assertion, what must it disclose?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Nothing; the indefinite reinvestment assertion eliminates all disclosure requirements

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the name and country of the foreign subsidiary

choices.label: C
choices.text: The fair value of the foreign subsidiary's net assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: The cumulative amount of the temporary difference and a statement that a deferred tax liability has not been recognized, plus the amount of the unrecognized DTL if determination is practicable

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-30-50-2, when the indefinite reversal (reinvestment) exception is applied to a foreign subsidiary, the parent must disclose: (1) a description of the types of temporary differences for which a DTL has not been recognized and the events that would cause the differences to become taxable, (2) the cumulative amount of each type of temporary difference, and (3) the amount of the unrecognized deferred tax liability if determination is practicable (or a statement that determination is not practicable).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-30-50-2により、海外子会社に無期限再投資の例外を適用する場合、親会社は次を開示する：(1) DTLを認識していない一時差異の種類と課税対象となる事象の説明、(2) 各種類の一時差異の累積額、(3) 算定可能な場合は未認識DTLの額（算定困難な場合はその旨）。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax on Equity Component of Cash Flow Hedge Reclassification
questions.stem: Westbrook Corp. has a cumulative gain of $300,000 in AOCI from an effective cash flow hedge, with a related deferred tax liability of $63,000 recorded in AOCI (21% rate). During the year, $100,000 of the hedge gain is reclassified from AOCI into earnings as the hedged forecasted transaction affects income. What is the tax effect of the reclassification?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reclassify $21,000 of deferred tax from AOCI to income tax expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record $21,000 of new DTL in AOCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reclassify $63,000 of deferred tax from AOCI to income tax expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: No tax effect until the hedge is fully settled

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-20-45-11, when amounts previously recognized in OCI are reclassified into earnings, the related tax effects are also reclassified from AOCI to income tax expense (continuing operations). The $100,000 gain reclassified into earnings carries $100,000 × 21% = $21,000 of deferred tax, which moves from AOCI to income tax expense. This is the intraperiod allocation 'backwards tracing' on reclassification.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-20-45-11により、OCIで認識した金額を損益へ振り替える際、関連する税効果もAOCIから法人税費用（継続事業）へ振り替える。損益へ振替えた利益$100,000に対応する繰延税金 $100,000 × 21% = $21,000がAOCIから法人税費用へ移動する。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Asset from Accrued Compensation (Bonus)
questions.stem: Oakdale Corp. accrues a $400,000 year-end bonus liability for book purposes in Year 1. The bonus is not paid within 2.5 months after year-end, so it is not deductible for tax until paid in Year 2. The enacted tax rate is 21%. What deferred tax asset, if any, should Oakdale recognize at the end of Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $0, because compensation is a permanent difference

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $84,000

questions.explanation: The accrued bonus is expensed for book in Year 1 but is not deductible for tax until paid in Year 2 (it fails the 2.5-month rule). The bonus liability has a book basis of $400,000 and a tax basis of $0, creating a deductible temporary difference (a future deductible amount when paid). DTA = $400,000 × 21% = $84,000.
questions.explanationJa: 未払賞与は帳簿上Year 1に費用計上するが、2.5ヶ月ルールを満たさず税務上はYear 2の支払時まで控除不可。賞与負債は帳簿価額$400,000・税務基準額$0で将来減算一時差異。DTA = $400,000 × 21% = $84,000。

questions.subtopic: Backwards Tracing Prohibition Under US GAAP
questions.stem: Under US GAAP (ASC 740), a deferred tax asset was originally established in OCI for an item. In a later period the enacted tax rate changes, altering the measurement of that deferred tax asset. Where is the effect of the rate change on that deferred tax asset recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In OCI, tracing back to where the DTA originated

choices.label: B
choices.text: Directly in retained earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: In income from continuing operations, even though the DTA originated in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: Allocated proportionally between OCI and continuing operations

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-45-15, the effect of a change in tax laws or rates on deferred tax assets and liabilities is recognized in income from continuing operations in the period of enactment, regardless of where the deferred tax originally arose. US GAAP generally prohibits 'backwards tracing' (with limited exceptions), so the rate-change effect on an OCI-originated DTA still flows through continuing operations, not OCI.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-45-15により、税法・税率変更の繰延税金資産・負債への影響は、当初の発生場所に関わらず制定期間の継続事業の損益で認識する。US GAAPは原則として「バックワード・トレーシング」を禁止する（限定的例外あり）ため、OCI由来のDTAへの税率変更の影響も継続事業の損益を通る。

questions.subtopic: Effective Tax Rate Reconciliation - Solve for Permanent Difference
questions.stem: Brighton Corp. reports pretax book income of $4,000,000 and total income tax expense of $798,000. The statutory rate is 21%, and there are no temporary differences. What net permanent (book-tax) item is implied by the effective tax rate?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $42,000 of net nondeductible expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: $200,000 of net nondeductible expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: $798,000 of net tax-exempt income

choices.label: D
choices.text: $200,000 of net tax-exempt income

questions.explanation: Tax computed at the statutory rate on book income = $4,000,000 × 21% = $840,000. Actual income tax expense is $798,000, which is $42,000 less than the statutory amount. With no temporary differences, taxable income = $798,000 / 21% = $3,800,000, which is $200,000 below book income of $4,000,000. Taxable income falling below book income means net favorable permanent items, specifically $200,000 of net tax-exempt income. Check: $200,000 × 21% = $42,000, exactly the favorable difference between statutory and actual tax. The implied permanent item is $200,000 of net tax-exempt income.
questions.explanationJa: 法定税率での税 = $4,000,000 × 21% = $840,000。実際の法人税費用$798,000はそれより$42,000少ない。一時差異がないため課税所得 = $798,000 ÷ 21% = $3,800,000で、帳簿利益$4,000,000を$200,000下回る。課税所得が帳簿利益を下回るのは純有利な永久項目、すなわち$200,000の純非課税所得を意味する。検証：$200,000 × 21% = $42,000で、法定税と実税の有利差と一致。したがって含意される永久項目は$200,000の純非課税所得。

questions.subtopic: Disclosure Threshold for Effective Rate Reconciliation
questions.stem: Under ASC 740-10-50-12, a public business entity must present a reconciliation of the reported income tax expense to the amount computed at the statutory rate. Individual reconciling items must be disclosed when they exceed what threshold of the statutory tax amount?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 5% of the amount computed by applying the statutory rate to pretax income

choices.label: B
choices.text: 1% of pretax income

choices.label: C
choices.text: 10% of net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Any amount, regardless of size, must be separately disclosed

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-50-12 and SEC Regulation S-X Rule 4-08, reconciling items in the effective tax rate reconciliation are individually disclosed when they exceed 5 percent of the amount computed by multiplying pretax income by the statutory federal tax rate. Reconciling items below this threshold may be aggregated. ASU 2023-09 expands disaggregation requirements, but the 5 percent reconciling-item threshold for separate presentation is the long-standing benchmark.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-50-12およびSEC規則S-X 4-08(h)により、実効税率調整の調整項目は、税引前利益に法定（連邦）税率を乗じた金額の5%を超える場合に個別開示する。この基準未満の項目は合算可能。（ASU 2023-09は細分化要件を拡充するが、個別表示の5%基準は従来の指標。）

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax on Depreciation Reversal Year
questions.stem: Langley Corp. bought an asset for $600,000 with a 3-year book life (straight-line, $200,000/year) and 3-year tax life with depreciation of $400,000, $150,000, and $50,000 in Years 1, 2, and 3. The rate is 21%. What is the deferred tax liability balance at the END of Year 2?
questions.explanation: Cumulative book depreciation through Year 2 = $200,000 + $200,000 = $400,000; book carrying amount = $600,000 − $400,000 = $200,000. Cumulative tax depreciation through Year 2 = $400,000 + $150,000 = $550,000; tax basis = $600,000 − $550,000 = $50,000. Temporary difference = $200,000 − $50,000 = $150,000 (book carrying amount exceeds tax basis, a future taxable amount). DTL balance at the end of Year 2 = $150,000 × 21% = $31,500.

questions.stem: Grafton Corp. incurs a $400,000 NOL in Year 3 that, under a special provision, may be carried back to Year 1, when taxable income was $500,000 and tax of $105,000 was paid at a 21% rate. Year 1's rate was 21%. What income tax refund receivable should Grafton record in Year 3 for the carryback?
questions.explanation: When an NOL is carried back, the benefit is the refund of taxes previously paid in the carryback year, computed at that year's rate. The $400,000 NOL offsets Year 1 income, generating a refund of $400,000 × 21% = $84,000. This is recognized as an income tax refund receivable (a current asset) and an income tax benefit in Year 3. The carryback benefit is certain (taxes were actually paid), so no valuation allowance applies.
questions.explanationJa: NOLを繰り戻す場合、便益は繰戻年度に過去支払った税金の還付で、その年度の税率で計算する。$400,000のNOLがYear 1の所得を相殺し、$400,000 × 21% = $84,000の還付が生じる。Year 3に法人税還付未収金（流動資産）と法人税ベネフィットを認識。繰戻便益は確実（実際に納税済み）のため評価性引当金は不要。

questions.subtopic: Naked Credit - Indefinite-Lived DTL and DTA Realization
questions.stem: Crestline Corp. has a deferred tax asset of $500,000 from an NOL carryforward that, post-2017, has an indefinite life but is subject to the 80% taxable income limitation. It also has a deferred tax liability of $300,000 arising from an indefinite-lived intangible (a 'naked credit'). In assessing the need for a valuation allowance on the DTA, how should the indefinite-lived DTL be treated as a source of taxable income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The indefinite-lived DTL cannot serve as a source of taxable income under any circumstances

choices.label: B
choices.text: The DTL must first be reduced to zero before any DTA is recognized

choices.label: C
choices.text: The DTL and DTA must be netted and only the excess is reported

choices.label: D
choices.text: The indefinite-lived DTL can serve as a source of future taxable income to support the indefinite-lived NOL DTA (subject to the 80% limitation)

questions.explanation: After ASU 2016-01 and the TCJA, post-2017 NOL carryforwards have an indefinite life. A deferred tax liability related to an indefinite-lived intangible (which previously could not be scheduled to reverse and was a 'naked credit') CAN now be considered a source of future taxable income to support realization of indefinite-lived DTAs such as post-2017 NOLs, subject to the 80% limitation. This relieves part of what was historically a stranded valuation allowance problem.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-01およびTCJA以降、2017年以降のNOL繰越は無期限。従来は解消時期を予定できず「ネイキッド・クレジット」とされた無期限無形資産関連のDTLを、現在は無期限DTA（2017年以降NOL等）の実現を支える将来課税所得の源泉として、80%制限を条件に考慮できる。これにより従来の評価性引当金の滞留問題が一部緩和された。

questions.stem: Under ASC 740-10-30, which of the following is described as a piece of negative evidence that is difficult to overcome when assessing the realizability of deferred tax assets?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A cumulative loss in recent years (typically the current and two preceding years)

choices.label: B
choices.text: A strong history of taxable income in the carryforward period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Existing contracts that will produce future taxable income

choices.label: D
choices.text: An excess of appreciated asset value over the tax basis of net assets

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-21, a cumulative loss in recent years (generally measured over the current year and the two preceding years) is significant negative evidence that is difficult to overcome and weighs heavily against recognizing deferred tax assets without a valuation allowance. A strong history of taxable income, existing profitable contracts, and appreciated asset values in excess of tax basis are all forms of positive evidence supporting realizability.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-21により、近年（通常は当期および直前2年）の累積損失は、克服が困難な重要なマイナスの証拠であり、評価性引当金なしでのDTA認識に強く不利に働く。課税所得の強固な実績、既存の収益性のある契約、税務基準額を上回る資産の含み益は、いずれも実現可能性を支えるプラスの証拠である。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax on Section 179 / Bonus Depreciation
questions.stem: Dunmore Corp. acquires qualifying equipment for $300,000 in Year 1 and elects 100% bonus depreciation, deducting the entire $300,000 for tax in Year 1. For book purposes the equipment is depreciated straight-line over 5 years ($60,000/year). The enacted tax rate is 21%. What is the deferred tax liability at the end of Year 1?
questions.explanation: Book depreciation Year 1 = $60,000; book carrying amount = $300,000 − $60,000 = $240,000. Tax depreciation Year 1 = $300,000 (100% bonus); tax basis = $300,000 − $300,000 = $0. Temporary difference = $240,000 − $0 = $240,000 (book basis exceeds tax basis, future taxable). DTL = $240,000 × 21% = $50,400.

questions.subtopic: Two Temporary Differences Net Tax Expense
questions.stem: Ashford Corp. has pretax book income of $1,200,000. During the year, a taxable temporary difference of $300,000 originated (book income exceeded taxable income) and a deductible temporary difference of $80,000 originated (taxable income exceeded book income). There are no permanent differences. The tax rate is 25%. What is total income tax expense?
questions.explanation: Because there are no permanent differences, total income tax expense equals pretax book income times the enacted rate: $1,200,000 × 25% = $300,000. Verification by components: Taxable income = $1,200,000 − $300,000 + $80,000 = $980,000; current tax = $980,000 × 25% = $245,000. Deferred tax: DTL on $300,000 = $75,000 (expense); DTA on $80,000 = $20,000 (benefit); net deferred expense = $75,000 − $20,000 = $55,000. Total = $245,000 + $55,000 = $300,000.

questions.subtopic: Measurement Objective of ASC 740 - No Discounting
questions.stem: Belmont Corp. has a deferred tax liability that will reverse evenly over the next 10 years. The amount of the future taxable temporary difference is $1,000,000 and the enacted tax rate is 21%. The company's discount rate is 8%. At what amount should the deferred tax liability be measured?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Present value of $210,000 discounted at 8%

choices.label: C
choices.text: Present value of $1,000,000 discounted at 8%

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-8, deferred tax assets and liabilities are NOT discounted. They are measured at the undiscounted amount of the temporary difference multiplied by the enacted tax rate expected to apply when the difference reverses. DTL = $1,000,000 × 21% = $210,000, recorded at face (undiscounted) value regardless of the timing of reversal or the company's discount rate.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-8により、繰延税金資産・負債は割引しない。一時差異の割引前金額に解消時に適用される制定税率を乗じて測定する。DTL = $1,000,000 × 21% = $210,000を、解消時期や割引率に関わらず額面（割引前）で計上する。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax on Capitalized R&E Costs (Post-2022 Section 174)
questions.stem: For tax years beginning after 2021, IRC Section 174 requires domestic research and experimental (R&E) expenditures to be capitalized and amortized over 5 years. Marshall Corp. incurs $500,000 of domestic R&E in Year 1, expenses it fully for book purposes, but for tax deducts only $50,000 in Year 1 (half-year of a 5-year amortization). The rate is 21%. What deferred tax item arises at the end of Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $94,500

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $94,500

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $105,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No deferred tax because R&E is a permanent difference

questions.explanation: Book expenses the full $500,000 in Year 1 (book basis of any R&E asset = $0). For tax, $500,000 is capitalized and only $50,000 is amortized in Year 1, leaving a tax basis of $500,000 − $50,000 = $450,000. The tax basis ($450,000) exceeds the book basis ($0) by $450,000, a deductible temporary difference (future tax amortization will be deducted, producing future deductible amounts). DTA = $450,000 × 21% = $94,500.
questions.explanationJa: 帳簿はYear 1に$500,000全額費用化（R&E資産の帳簿基準額$0）。税務は$500,000を資産化しYear 1に$50,000のみ償却、税務基準額 = $500,000 − $50,000 = $450,000。税務基準額が帳簿基準額を$450,000上回る将来減算一時差異（将来の税務償却で控除）。DTA = $450,000 × 21% = $94,500。

questions.subtopic: Change in Valuation Allowance Acquired in Business Combination
questions.stem: One year after a business combination, Acquirer Corp. releases (reverses) an $80,000 valuation allowance that had been established at the acquisition date against an acquired deferred tax asset. Under current guidance (ASU 2010 amendments to ASC 805-740), how is this subsequent release recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a reduction of goodwill

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a reduction of income tax expense in the period the valuation allowance is released

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an increase to additional paid-in capital

choices.label: D
choices.text: Retrospectively, by adjusting the acquisition-date goodwill

questions.explanation: Under current guidance (ASC 805-740-45-2/-4), changes to a valuation allowance for acquired deferred tax assets that occur AFTER the measurement period (or that do not result from new information about acquisition-date facts) are recognized in income tax expense (or as an adjustment to APIC or other applicable accounts where appropriate), NOT as an adjustment to goodwill. The historical rule of reducing goodwill first was eliminated. The $80,000 release reduces income tax expense in the current period.
questions.explanationJa: 現行指針（ASC 805-740-45-2/-4）では、取得したDTAの評価性引当金の変更が測定期間後（または取得日事実に関する新情報によらない場合）に生じた場合、のれんではなく法人税費用（または適切な場合はAPIC等）で認識する。のれんを先に減額する旧ルールは廃止された。$80,000の取崩しは当期の法人税費用を減少させる。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax on Unrealized Gain - Trading Securities
questions.stem: Kendrick Corp. holds equity securities measured at fair value through net income. At year-end the securities have a cost of $400,000 and a fair value of $470,000; the $70,000 unrealized gain is recognized in net income for book purposes. For tax, the gain is not recognized until the securities are sold. The rate is 21%. What deferred tax item arises, and where is the offset recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $14,700, offset in OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $14,700, offset in income tax expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $14,700, offset in income tax expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: No deferred tax because unrealized gains are permanent

questions.explanation: The unrealized gain raises the book basis of the securities above the tax basis by $70,000 — a taxable temporary difference (future taxable amount when sold). DTL = $70,000 × 21% = $14,700. Because the unrealized gain on these securities runs through net income (not OCI), under intraperiod allocation the related deferred tax effect is recognized in income tax expense (continuing operations), not OCI. This contrasts with AFS debt securities, whose unrealized gains and related tax effect are recognized in OCI.
questions.explanationJa: 未実現利益により有価証券の帳簿基準額が税務基準額を$70,000上回る将来加算一時差異（売却時に課税）。DTL = $70,000 × 21% = $14,700。これらの有価証券の未実現利益は純利益を通る（OCIではない）ため、期間内配分により関連繰延税効果は法人税費用（継続事業）で認識する。OCIで認識されるAFS負債証券とは対照的。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Liability Recognition on Equity Method - Foreign Corporate Joint Venture
questions.stem: Under ASC 740-30-25, the indefinite reversal exception (allowing nonrecognition of a deferred tax liability on undistributed earnings) extends to which of the following?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Undistributed earnings of a domestic 30%-owned equity investee expected to be distributed soon

choices.label: B
choices.text: Temporary differences related to depreciable assets of a wholly owned domestic subsidiary

choices.label: C
choices.text: Any equity method investment regardless of duration or distribution intent

choices.label: D
choices.text: Undistributed earnings of a foreign corporate joint venture that is essentially permanent in duration

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-30-25-18, the indefinite reversal exception applies to (1) undistributed earnings of foreign subsidiaries and foreign corporate joint ventures that are essentially permanent in duration, and (2) certain other limited situations. For a foreign corporate joint venture essentially permanent in duration, a parent need not recognize a DTL on undistributed earnings if it can assert indefinite reinvestment. The exception does not extend to a domestic equity investee whose earnings are expected to be distributed soon, to ordinary depreciable asset basis differences, or to all equity investments without regard to duration or distribution intent.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-30-25-18により、無期限繰延の例外は、(1) 実質的に存続が永久的な海外子会社・海外コーポレート・ジョイントベンチャーの未分配利益、(2) その他限定的状況に適用される。存続が永久的な海外JVについて無期限再投資を主張できれば、未分配利益にDTLを認識しなくてよい。早期分配が見込まれる国内投資先、通常の減価償却資産の基準差異、存続期間や分配意図を問わないすべての投資には及ばない。

questions.subtopic: Interim Reporting - Loss in Early Quarter Limited Benefit
questions.stem: In the first quarter, Ridgeway Corp. has a pretax loss of $200,000. Its estimated annual effective tax rate is 25%, and management expects full-year income; the tax benefit of the Q1 loss is expected to be realized. What income tax benefit should Ridgeway report for Q1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $50,000 benefit

choices.label: C
choices.text: $200,000 benefit

choices.label: D
choices.text: $50,000 expense

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-270-30-2, the interim tax provision applies the estimated annual effective tax rate to year-to-date pretax income (or loss). Year-to-date through Q1 is a $200,000 loss. Tax benefit = $200,000 × 25% = $50,000. Because management expects full-year income and the benefit of the loss is more likely than not realizable, the $50,000 benefit is recognized in Q1 (no limitation applies). This produces an income tax benefit of $50,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-270-30-2により、中間期の税金引当は見積年間実効税率を年度累計の税引前損益に適用する。Q1までの累計は$200,000の損失。税ベネフィット = $200,000 × 25% = $50,000。通年で利益が見込まれ損失の便益がmore likely than notで実現可能なため、$50,000のベネフィットをQ1で認識（制限なし）。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Asset - Accrued Vacation/Compensated Absences
questions.stem: Holloway Corp. accrues $150,000 of compensated absences (vacation) liability for book purposes at year-end. For tax, vacation pay is deductible only when paid; none was paid by year-end. The rate is 21%. Which statement correctly describes the deferred tax effect?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A deferred tax liability of $31,500 arises from a taxable temporary difference

choices.label: B
choices.text: A deferred tax asset of $31,500 arises from a deductible temporary difference

choices.label: C
choices.text: No deferred tax because compensated absences are a permanent difference

choices.label: D
choices.text: A deferred tax asset of $150,000 arises

questions.explanation: The vacation liability is expensed for book in the current year but deductible for tax only when paid. The liability has a book basis of $150,000 and a tax basis of $0, creating a deductible temporary difference (a future deductible amount when the vacation is paid). DTA = $150,000 × 21% = $31,500.
questions.explanationJa: 有給休暇引当金は当期に帳簿費用化されるが、税務上は支払時のみ控除可能。負債は帳簿基準額$150,000・税務基準額$0で将来減算一時差異（支払時に控除）。DTA = $150,000 × 21% = $31,500。

questions.subtopic: Unrecognized Tax Benefit Presentation - Netting Against DTA
questions.stem: Under ASC 740-10-45-10B, when an entity has an unrecognized tax benefit and an available net operating loss carryforward, deferred tax asset, or other tax credit carryforward, how should the liability for the unrecognized tax benefit be presented?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Always as a separate noncurrent liability, never netted against any asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: Always as a reduction of goodwill

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a reduction of the related DTA or NOL/credit carryforward, to the extent the carryforward would be used to settle the position; otherwise as a liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a current liability regardless of the related carryforwards

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-45-10B (added by ASU 2013-11), an unrecognized tax benefit, or a portion of it, is presented as a reduction to a deferred tax asset for an NOL carryforward, a similar tax loss, or a tax credit carryforward when settlement of the position would use that carryforward. To the extent such a carryforward is not available at the reporting date (or is not used to settle), the liability is presented separately. This nets the liability against the related carryforward rather than grossing up the balance sheet.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-45-10B（ASU 2013-11で追加）により、ポジションの決済にNOL繰越・類似の税損失・税額控除繰越を使用する場合、未認識税務便益（の一部）はその繰延税金資産の減額として表示する。当該繰越が報告日に利用不能（または決済に使用されない）部分は、負債として別個に表示する。これにより貸借対照表を膨らませず、関連繰越と相殺する。

questions.subtopic: Effective Tax Rate Below Statutory Due to Tax Credit
questions.stem: Pemberton Corp. has pretax book income of $3,000,000, no permanent or temporary differences other than a $90,000 general business tax credit, and a statutory rate of 21%. What is Pemberton's effective tax rate?
questions.explanation: Tax before credit = $3,000,000 × 21% = $630,000. General business tax credit reduces tax dollar-for-dollar: $630,000 − $90,000 = $540,000 total income tax expense. Effective tax rate = $540,000 / $3,000,000 = 18.0%. The credit lowers the effective rate below the 21% statutory rate by 3 percentage points ($90,000 / $3,000,000 = 3%).

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax on Pension - Funded Status Difference
questions.stem: Fairmont Corp. sponsors a defined benefit pension plan. At year-end the plan is underfunded; the recognized net pension liability for book purposes is $500,000. For tax purposes, pension costs are deductible only when contributions are made, and the tax basis of the pension liability is $0. The rate is 21%. What deferred tax item relates to the net pension liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $105,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $105,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $500,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No deferred tax because pension funding is a permanent difference

questions.explanation: The net pension liability has a book basis of $500,000 and a tax basis of $0. A liability whose book basis exceeds its tax basis creates a deductible temporary difference (future tax deductions occur when contributions are eventually made/the obligation is settled). DTA = $500,000 × 21% = $105,000. (Portions arising through OCI, such as actuarial losses, carry their deferred tax in OCI; the deductible difference on the funded status produces a DTA either way.)
questions.explanationJa: 純年金負債は帳簿基準額$500,000・税務基準額$0。帳簿基準額が税務基準額を上回る負債は将来減算一時差異（将来の拠出/債務決済時に税務控除）。DTA = $500,000 × 21% = $105,000。（保険数理損失などOCIを通る部分の繰延税金はOCIに計上されるが、積立状況の控除差異はいずれにせよDTAを生む。）

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Asset from Capital Loss Carryforward
questions.stem: Whitfield Corp. realizes a $200,000 capital loss in Year 1. Tax law allows corporations to deduct capital losses only against capital gains, with a carryforward period. Whitfield has no current capital gains but expects $200,000 of capital gains in Year 2. The book loss was recognized in Year 1 income. The rate is 21%. How should Whitfield account for the capital loss carryforward?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize no DTA because capital losses are permanent differences

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize a DTA of $42,000, with a valuation allowance unless it is more likely than not that future capital gains will absorb the loss

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a DTA of $42,000 with no realizability assessment required

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a DTL of $42,000

questions.explanation: A capital loss carryforward creates a deductible temporary difference / carryforward, so a DTA of $200,000 × 21% = $42,000 is recognized. However, because corporate capital losses can only offset capital gains, realizability is assessed against the specific source of future capital gains. A valuation allowance is required unless it is more likely than not that sufficient future capital gains (of the right character) will be available. Here Whitfield expects $200,000 of capital gains in Year 2, which supports realization; absent that expectation, a valuation allowance would be needed.
questions.explanationJa: 資本損失の繰越は将来減算一時差異/繰越を生むため、DTA = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000を認識する。ただし法人の資本損失は資本利得としか相殺できないため、実現可能性は将来の資本利得という特定の源泉に対して評価する。同性質の十分な将来資本利得がmore likely than notで生じない限り評価性引当金が必要。本問はYear 2に$200,000の資本利得を見込むため実現を支持する。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Asset Recognition Without Valuation Allowance - Reversing DTL Source
questions.stem: Garrison Corp. has a deductible temporary difference of $400,000 (creating a gross DTA) that will reverse entirely in Year 4. It also has a taxable temporary difference of $400,000 (creating a DTL) that will reverse entirely in Year 4. Garrison has a history of losses and no other source of future taxable income. The rate is 21%. Is a valuation allowance required against the $84,000 gross DTA?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A full $84,000 valuation allowance is required because of the history of losses

choices.label: B
choices.text: A valuation allowance equal to half the DTA is required

choices.label: C
choices.text: No valuation allowance is needed because the reversing taxable temporary difference provides a source of taxable income in the same period the deductible difference reverses

choices.label: D
choices.text: No DTA may be recognized at all when the entity has a history of losses

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-18, one source of taxable income supporting DTA realization is the future reversal of existing taxable temporary differences. The $400,000 taxable temporary difference (DTL) reverses in Year 4 — the SAME period the $400,000 deductible difference reverses — generating $400,000 of taxable income that the deductible difference can offset. Because the reversals match in timing and amount, the DTA is realizable without regard to other income sources, and NO valuation allowance is needed despite the loss history. DTA = $84,000 is recognized in full.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-18により、DTA実現を支える源泉の一つは既存の課税一時差異の将来の解消。$400,000の課税一時差異（DTL）はYear 4に解消し、これは$400,000の減算差異が解消するのと同じ期間で、減算差異が相殺できる$400,000の課税所得を生む。解消の時期と金額が一致するため、他の所得源泉に関わらずDTAは実現可能で、損失履歴があっても評価性引当金は不要。DTA $84,000を全額認識する。

questions.stem: Which of the following items creates a deductible temporary difference that results in a deferred tax asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A gain on an installment sale recognized currently for book purposes but reported for tax purposes as cash is collected

choices.label: B
choices.text: Tax depreciation in excess of book depreciation on equipment

choices.label: C
choices.text: An accrued litigation loss recognized for book purposes that is not deductible for tax purposes until paid

choices.label: D
choices.text: A prepaid expense deducted on the tax return in the year paid but expensed for book purposes in a later year

questions.explanation: An accrued litigation loss reduces book income now but will reduce taxable income only when paid in the future, creating a deductible temporary difference and a deferred tax asset. The installment sale gain, excess tax depreciation, and prepaid expense deducted currently for tax all cause book income to exceed taxable income initially and will produce taxable amounts in the future, so each creates a taxable temporary difference and a deferred tax liability.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-20では、将来減算一時差異（将来の課税所得を減少させる差異）が繰延税金資産（DTA）を生じさせる。訴訟損失の引当は帳簿上先に費用認識され、税務上は支払時に損金算入されるため、将来減算一時差異となりDTAが生じる。割賦販売益の先行計上、税務上の加速償却、税務上先行控除した前払費用は、いずれも帳簿利益が課税所得を先行して上回る将来加算一時差異であり、繰延税金負債（DTL）を生じさせる。

questions.subtopic: DTA from Allowance for Credit Losses
questions.stem: Corbett Corp. records an allowance for credit losses of $250,000 for financial reporting purposes. For tax purposes, bad debts are deductible only when specific accounts are written off (direct write-off method), and no accounts have been written off to date. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What amount of deferred tax asset should Corbett recognize related to the allowance?
questions.explanation: The allowance reduces the book basis of receivables below their tax basis by $250,000, a deductible temporary difference. The deferred tax asset is $250,000 × 25% = $62,500. $250,000 is the gross temporary difference, not its tax effect; $50,000 incorrectly applies a 20% rate; $0 ignores the temporary difference entirely.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-5に基づき、繰延税金は一時差異に制定税率を乗じて測定する。貸倒引当金$250,000は帳簿上先に費用化されるが、税務上は個別償却時まで損金算入できないため将来減算一時差異となる。DTA = $250,000 × 25% = $62,500。$250,000は税効果前の差異総額、$50,000は誤った税率20%を適用した値である。

questions.subtopic: Current Tax Payable with Multiple Adjustments
questions.stem: Delgado Corp. reports pretax financial income of $900,000 for Year 1. The following items are included in or related to that amount: (1) premiums of $40,000 on officers' life insurance where Delgado is the beneficiary (nondeductible), (2) tax depreciation exceeds book depreciation by $140,000, and (3) rent of $60,000 received in advance is taxable on receipt but deferred for book purposes. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What is Delgado's current income tax payable for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Taxable income = $900,000 + $40,000 nondeductible premiums − $140,000 excess tax depreciation + $60,000 advance rent taxable currently = $860,000. Current tax payable = $860,000 × 25% = $215,000. $225,000 applies the rate to book income with no adjustments; $285,000 results from adding rather than subtracting the depreciation difference; $190,000 omits the insurance and rent adjustments.
questions.explanationJa: 当期税金費用（未払法人税）は課税所得×制定税率で計算する（ASC 740-10-30-2）。課税所得 = $900,000 +（損金不算入の役員保険料）$40,000 −（税務上の超過償却）$140,000 +（前受家賃の即時課税）$60,000 = $860,000。未払税金 = $860,000 × 25% = $215,000。$225,000は調整を一切行わない誤り、$285,000は減価償却差異の符号を逆にした誤り。

questions.subtopic: Total Tax Expense - Current Plus Deferred
questions.stem: During Year 1, Ellsworth Corp. computed current income tax payable of $264,000. During the year, net taxable temporary differences increased by $192,000, and the enacted tax rate is 25%. There were no other items affecting the tax provision. What is Ellsworth's total income tax expense for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Deferred tax expense equals the increase in net taxable temporary differences times the enacted rate: $192,000 × 25% = $48,000. Total tax expense = current $264,000 + deferred $48,000 = $312,000. $264,000 is only the current portion; $216,000 incorrectly subtracts the deferred component; $456,000 incorrectly adds the gross temporary difference rather than its tax effect.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-10-1のとおり、法人税等費用は当期税金費用と繰延税金費用の合計である。繰延税金費用 = 将来加算一時差異の増加$192,000 × 25% = $48,000。総税金費用 = $264,000 + $48,000 = $312,000。$264,000は当期分のみ、$456,000は一時差異の総額（税効果前）を加算した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Rate Change Effect on Deferred Tax Asset
questions.stem: Farnsworth Corp. has a deferred tax asset of $180,000 arising from a $600,000 deductible temporary difference measured at the currently enacted rate of 30%. In December of the current year, a new tax law is enacted reducing the rate to 25% for all future years. What is the effect of the rate change on income tax expense in the period of enactment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase income tax expense by $30,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease income tax expense by $30,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase income tax expense by $150,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No effect until the new rate becomes effective

questions.explanation: The deferred tax asset must be remeasured at the newly enacted rate: $600,000 × 25% = $150,000, a decrease of $30,000 from the recorded $180,000. Writing down a deferred tax asset increases income tax expense by $30,000 in the period of enactment. A $30,000 decrease in expense would occur only if a deferred tax liability were being remeasured downward; $150,000 is the remeasured asset balance, not the change; deferring recognition until the effective date contradicts ASC 740's enactment-date requirement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-35-4により、税率変更の影響は制定（enactment）された期の継続事業に係る税金費用に計上する。DTAは$600,000 × 25% = $150,000に再測定され、既存の$180,000から$30,000減少する。DTAの減額は税金費用を$30,000増加させる。発効日まで待つという処理は制定日基準に反する。

questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 2, Granville Corp. had a gross deferred tax asset of $400,000 and a valuation allowance of $90,000. At the end of Year 2, based on updated projections of future taxable income, Granville determines that a valuation allowance of $140,000 is required (the gross deferred tax asset is unchanged). How should the Year 2 change in the valuation allowance be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A $140,000 increase in income tax expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: A $50,000 decrease in income tax expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: A $50,000 increase in income tax expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: A $90,000 increase in income tax expense

questions.explanation: Only the change in the valuation allowance during the period affects tax expense: $140,000 required − $90,000 existing = $50,000 increase, which increases income tax expense. $140,000 is the ending balance, not the change; a decrease in expense would result from reducing the allowance; $90,000 is the beginning balance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-45-20により、評価性引当金の期中変動額は原則として継続事業の税金費用に計上する。必要額$140,000 − 期首残高$90,000 = $50,000の引当金増加であり、税金費用が$50,000増加する。$140,000は期末残高であって変動額ではない点に注意。

questions.subtopic: NOL Utilization - Mixed Pre-2018 and Post-2017 Vintages
questions.stem: Hadley Corp. enters Year 5 with two federal net operating loss carryforwards: $100,000 generated in 2016 (pre-2018 NOL, not subject to the 80% limitation) and $300,000 generated in 2021 (post-2017 NOL, subject to the 80% limitation). Year 5 taxable income before any NOL deduction is $250,000. Under current federal tax law, pre-2018 NOLs are used first and are not limited. What is Hadley's Year 5 taxable income after the NOL deductions?
questions.explanation: The pre-2018 NOL of $100,000 offsets income without limitation, leaving $150,000. The post-2017 NOL deduction is limited to 80% of the remaining taxable income: 80% × $150,000 = $120,000. Taxable income = $250,000 − $100,000 − $120,000 = $30,000. $0 ignores the 80% limitation; $50,000 incorrectly applies the 80% limit to the full $250,000 for both vintages; $150,000 uses only the pre-2018 NOL.
questions.explanationJa: TCJA後の税法では、2018年以後発生のNOLは課税所得の80%まで（無期限繰越）、2018年前発生のNOLは制限なし（20年で失効）で控除できる。2016年分$100,000を全額控除後の残額$150,000に対し、2021年分は80% × $150,000 = $120,000が控除上限。課税所得 = $250,000 − $100,000 − $120,000 = $30,000。$0は80%制限の見落とし、$50,000は$250,000全体に80%制限を適用した誤り。

questions.stem: Ingram Corp. takes a tax position claiming a $600,000 benefit and concludes the position is more likely than not to be sustained. Management estimates the following possible outcomes and individual probabilities upon settlement with the taxing authority: $600,000 (30% likely), $400,000 (25% likely), and $200,000 (45% likely). What amount of tax benefit should Ingram recognize?
questions.explanation: ASC 740 measures a recognized tax position at the largest benefit that is greater than 50% likely of being realized on a cumulative-probability basis. Cumulative probabilities: $600,000 → 30%; $400,000 → 30% + 25% = 55%. Because 55% exceeds 50%, $400,000 is recognized. $600,000 reaches only 30% cumulative probability; $370,000 is an expected-value calculation, which ASC 740 does not permit; $200,000 understates the benefit because a larger amount already exceeds the 50% threshold.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-7により、認識基準（more-likely-than-not）を満たした税務ポジションは、累積確率が50%を超える最大の便益額で測定する。累積確率は$600,000で30%、$400,000で55%（30%+25%）となり、50%を超える最大額は$400,000。期待値（$370,000）による測定はASC 740では認められない。

questions.subtopic: Interim Provision with Revised Annual Rate
questions.stem: In the first quarter of Year 1, Jasper Corp. estimated its annual effective tax rate at 30% and reported Q1 pretax income of $100,000, recording $30,000 of income tax expense. In the second quarter, Jasper revises its estimated annual effective tax rate to 26%. Q2 pretax income is $250,000. What income tax expense should Jasper report for the second quarter?
questions.explanation: Under the integral approach, the year-to-date provision is computed at the current estimated annual rate and the previously recorded amount is subtracted: ($100,000 + $250,000) × 26% = $91,000 year-to-date, less $30,000 recorded in Q1 = $61,000 for Q2. $65,000 applies 26% only to Q2 income, ignoring the catch-up adjustment for Q1; $91,000 is the year-to-date total, not the Q2 amount; $75,000 applies the old 30% rate to Q2 income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-270の一体（integral）アプローチでは、見積年間実効税率の改訂はYTDベースで反映する。YTD税金費用 = ($100,000 + $250,000) × 26% = $91,000。Q1計上済み$30,000を差し引き、Q2の税金費用は$61,000。これによりQ1の旧税率30%との差額がQ2でキャッチアップ調整される。$65,000はQ2単独に26%を乗じただけの誤り。

questions.subtopic: Net-of-Tax Presentation of OCI Pension Loss
questions.stem: Kingsley Corp. incurs an actuarial loss of $240,000 on its defined benefit pension plan during Year 1, recognized in other comprehensive income. The loss is not currently deductible for tax purposes. The enacted tax rate is 25%. At what amount should the actuarial loss be reported in other comprehensive income, net of tax?
questions.explanation: Items of other comprehensive income are reported net of their tax effects. The deferred tax benefit allocated to OCI is $240,000 × 25% = $60,000, so the net-of-tax loss is $240,000 − $60,000 = $180,000. $240,000 is the gross loss; $60,000 is the tax effect alone; $192,000 incorrectly applies a 20% rate.
questions.explanationJa: 期間内税金配分（intraperiod tax allocation, ASC 740-20-45）により、OCI項目はその税効果を控除した純額で表示する。年金数理差損に係る税効果（DTA）= $240,000 × 25% = $60,000。OCI表示額 = $240,000 − $60,000 = $180,000（純損失）。$240,000は税効果考慮前の総額である。

questions.subtopic: Nontaxable Business Combination - DTL on Fair Value Step-Up
questions.stem: In a nontaxable business combination, Lockhart Corp. acquires a customer relationship intangible with an acquisition-date fair value of $800,000. Because the transaction is nontaxable, the asset's tax basis carries over at $500,000. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What deferred tax amount should Lockhart recognize for this asset in acquisition accounting?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A deferred tax asset of $75,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: No deferred tax, because the difference arose in a business combination

choices.label: C
choices.text: A deferred tax liability of $200,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: A deferred tax liability of $75,000

questions.explanation: The book basis ($800,000) exceeds the carryover tax basis ($500,000) by $300,000, a taxable temporary difference. A deferred tax liability of $300,000 × 25% = $75,000 is recognized at the acquisition date, which increases goodwill. A deferred tax asset would arise only if tax basis exceeded book basis; $200,000 incorrectly applies the rate to the full fair value; the business-combination exception applies to goodwill itself, not to identifiable assets.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-740-25-3により、企業結合で取得した識別可能資産・負債の帳簿価額と税務基準額の差異には繰延税金を認識する（のれん自体を除く）。将来加算一時差異 = $800,000 − $500,000 = $300,000、DTL = $300,000 × 25% = $75,000。このDTLの認識はのれんを増加させる。非課税取引のため税務基準額は$500,000のまま引き継がれる点がポイント。

questions.subtopic: Excess Tax Benefit on Option Exercise
questions.stem: Marlowe Corp. granted nonqualified stock options and recognized cumulative book compensation expense of $90,000 over the vesting period, recording a corresponding deferred tax asset at the 25% enacted rate. Upon exercise, the intrinsic value (and the tax deduction) is $150,000. Under current GAAP, how should the difference between the actual tax benefit and the previously recorded deferred tax asset be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a $15,000 credit to additional paid-in capital

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a $37,500 reduction of income tax expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a $15,000 reduction of income tax expense in the period of exercise

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a $22,500 credit to retained earnings

questions.explanation: The actual tax benefit is $150,000 × 25% = $37,500, while the deferred tax asset previously recorded was $90,000 × 25% = $22,500. The $15,000 excess tax benefit is recognized in income tax expense (a discrete-period benefit) under ASU 2016-09, not in additional paid-in capital. $37,500 is the total benefit, not the excess; crediting APIC reflects superseded guidance; retained earnings is never the destination for this item.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09（ASC 718-740）により、株式報酬の超過税務便益（windfall）はAPICではなく損益計算書の法人税等費用で認識する。実際の税務便益 = $150,000 × 25% = $37,500、計上済みDTA = $90,000 × 25% = $22,500。差額$15,000が行使期の税金費用の減額（離散項目）となる。APIC計上は改正前の旧基準の処理である。

questions.stem: Norcross Corp. granted restricted stock units and recognized cumulative book compensation expense of $200,000, recording a deferred tax asset at the 25% enacted rate. When the RSUs vest, the fair value of the shares delivered (and the tax deduction) is only $160,000 because the stock price declined. What is the effect of the vesting on Norcross's income tax expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No effect; the shortfall is charged to additional paid-in capital

choices.label: B
choices.text: A decrease of $40,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: An increase of $40,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: An increase of $10,000

questions.explanation: The deferred tax asset recorded was $200,000 × 25% = $50,000, but the actual tax benefit is only $160,000 × 25% = $40,000. The $10,000 shortfall is recognized as additional income tax expense in the period of vesting under ASU 2016-09; charging it to additional paid-in capital reflects superseded guidance. $40,000 is the gross deduction shortfall ($200,000 − $160,000) before applying the tax rate, not its $10,000 tax effect, so a $40,000 expense change overstates the effect.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09により、株式報酬の税務便益の不足額（shortfall）もAPICではなく税金費用で認識する。計上済みDTA = $200,000 × 25% = $50,000、実際の便益 = $160,000 × 25% = $40,000。不足額$10,000は権利確定期の税金費用の増加となる。$40,000は税効果前の控除差額の総額（$200,000−$160,000）であり、その税効果$10,000（×25%）と混同した値である。

questions.subtopic: Sources of Taxable Income for DTA Realization
questions.stem: Under ASC 740, all of the following are sources of taxable income that may support the realization of deferred tax assets EXCEPT:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Future reversals of existing taxable temporary differences

choices.label: B
choices.text: Taxable income in prior carryback years, if carryback is permitted

choices.label: C
choices.text: Projected increases in the enacted statutory tax rate in future years

choices.label: D
choices.text: Tax-planning strategies that would, if necessary, be implemented to realize a deferred tax asset

questions.explanation: ASC 740-10-30-18 identifies four sources of taxable income: future reversals of existing taxable temporary differences, future taxable income exclusive of reversals, taxable income in carryback years, and tax-planning strategies. A projected increase in the statutory rate is not a source of taxable income; deferred taxes are measured only at currently enacted rates, and anticipated rate changes cannot support realization.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-18は、DTAの実現可能性を支える課税所得の源泉として、(1)既存の将来加算一時差異の将来戻入れ、(2)将来の課税所得（戻入れを除く）、(3)繰戻し可能年度の課税所得、(4)タックスプランニング戦略、の4つを列挙している。将来の法定税率引上げの予測は課税所得の源泉ではなく、繰延税金は制定済み税率でのみ測定するため実現可能性の根拠にもならない。
