questions.stem: Under ASC 740, which of the following statements about a qualifying tax-planning strategy used to support realization of a deferred tax asset is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The strategy must already be part of management's operating plans regardless of tax consequences

choices.label: B
choices.text: The strategy must be prudent and feasible, and one that management ordinarily might not take but would take if necessary to prevent a carryforward from expiring unused

choices.label: C
choices.text: The strategy may be considered even if its implementation depends primarily on the actions of third parties

choices.label: D
choices.text: Expenses to implement the strategy are ignored when measuring the deferred tax asset that can be realized

questions.explanation: ASC 740-10-30-19 defines a tax-planning strategy as an action that is prudent and feasible, that an entity ordinarily might not take but would take to prevent an operating loss or tax credit carryforward from expiring unused, and that would result in realization of deferred tax assets. A strategy already embedded in operating plans is simply future taxable income, not a strategy; implementation must be primarily within management's control, not dependent on third parties; significant expenses to implement a strategy must be deducted from the resulting benefit.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-19により、タックスプランニング戦略とは、(a)慎重かつ実行可能で、(b)通常は実行しないが繰越欠損金等の失効を防ぐために必要なら実行し、(c)DTAの実現をもたらす行動をいう。実行が第三者の行動に依存するものは認められず、戦略の実施に要する重要な費用は実現便益から控除して測定する（ASC 740-10-30-19）。既に事業計画に含まれる行動は単なる将来課税所得であり「戦略」ではない。

questions.stem: Oakhurst Corp. deducts $800,000 on its tax return for a position that management concludes has only a 40% likelihood of being sustained upon examination, assuming the taxing authority has full knowledge of all relevant information. The enacted tax rate is 25%. How should Oakhurst account for this position in its financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize no tax benefit and record a $200,000 liability for the unrecognized tax benefit

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize a tax benefit of $80,000, the expected value of the position

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize the full $200,000 benefit because the deduction was claimed on a filed return

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a tax benefit of $100,000, half of the claimed amount

questions.explanation: Because the position does not meet the more-likely-than-not (greater than 50%) recognition threshold, no benefit may be recognized in the financial statements. Since the $800,000 × 25% = $200,000 benefit was taken on the return, the entity records a $200,000 liability for the unrecognized tax benefit. Expected-value measurement is not permitted; filing the return does not justify recognition; arbitrary partial recognition has no basis in ASC 740.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-6の認識基準（more-likely-than-not、50%超）を満たさない税務ポジションは、財務諸表上一切便益を認識できない。申告書上は$800,000 × 25% = $200,000の便益を享受しているため、その全額を未認識税務便益（UTB）の負債として計上する。期待値による測定や、申告済みであることを理由とした認識は認められない。

questions.stem: Pembroke Corp. began Year 3 with a liability for unrecognized tax benefits of $300,000. During Year 3: (1) new tax positions taken in the current year added $120,000, (2) settlements with taxing authorities resolved positions carried at $80,000, and (3) the statute of limitations lapsed on positions carried at $50,000. What is the ending balance of the liability for unrecognized tax benefits?
questions.explanation: Ending UTB = $300,000 beginning + $120,000 additions − $80,000 settlements − $50,000 statute lapses = $290,000. $340,000 omits the statute lapse; $370,000 omits the settlements; $420,000 includes only the additions and ignores both reductions.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-50-15Aは公開企業にUTBの期首から期末への増減表（rollforward）の開示を求めており、その構成は本問のとおり。期末UTB = 期首$300,000 + 当期ポジション追加$120,000 − 当局との解決$80,000 − 時効成立$50,000 = $290,000。時効成立（statute of limitations lapse）と当局との解決はいずれもUTB負債を取り崩し、便益認識につながる。

questions.subtopic: Interest Accrual on Unrecognized Tax Benefits
questions.stem: Quentin Corp. has a $400,000 unrecognized tax benefit relating to a position taken on its prior-year return, representing a tax underpayment under the tax law. The applicable statutory interest rate is 6% per year, and one full year has passed since the underpayment arose. Quentin's accounting policy is to classify interest on tax positions as income tax expense. What amount of interest should Quentin accrue for the year?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $0, because the position has not been examined

questions.explanation: ASC 740 requires an entity to accrue interest on the difference between the tax position taken on the return and the amount recognized in the financial statements, beginning when the underpayment would start accruing interest under the tax law: $400,000 × 6% × 1 year = $24,000. Waiting for examination is not permitted; $48,000 assumes two years; $12,000 assumes only half a year.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-56により、申告ポジションと財務諸表認識額の差額（税務上の過少納付額）には、税法上利息が発生し始める時点から利息を見積計上しなければならない。利息 = $400,000 × 6% × 1年 = $24,000。税務調査を待って計上する処理は認められない。利息・ペナルティの表示区分（税金費用か営業外か）は会計方針の選択であり、開示が必要（ASC 740-10-50-19）。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-16 (ASC 740), which of the following correctly describes the accounting for income tax consequences of intra-entity transfers of assets between members of a consolidated group?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Current and deferred tax consequences are recognized when the transfer occurs for all assets except inventory; for inventory, recognition is deferred until sale to an outside party

choices.label: B
choices.text: Tax consequences of all intra-entity transfers are deferred until the asset is sold to an outside party

choices.label: C
choices.text: Tax consequences of all intra-entity transfers are recognized immediately when the transfer occurs, with no exceptions

choices.label: D
choices.text: Tax consequences are recognized immediately only for transfers of inventory; all other transfers are deferred

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-16 requires recognition of the income tax consequences of an intra-entity transfer of an asset other than inventory when the transfer occurs. Inventory is the single exception: the seller's tax effects on intercompany inventory profit remain deferred until the inventory is sold to an outside party. Deferring everything reflects the pre-ASU model; recognizing everything immediately ignores the inventory exception; the exception runs in the opposite direction of immediate recognition for inventory only.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-16（ASC 740-10-25-3(e)の改正）により、棚卸資産以外の資産のグループ内移転に係る税効果（売手の当期税金、買手側の繰延税金）は移転時に認識する。棚卸資産のみが例外で、グループ内利益に係る税効果は外部への販売まで繰延べられる。改正前はすべての移転で繰延べる処理だったが、棚卸資産以外については廃止された。

questions.subtopic: DTL on Equity Method Earnings with DRD
questions.stem: Radcliffe Corp. owns 30% of a domestic investee and accounts for it under the equity method. Radcliffe's share of the investee's undistributed earnings for the year is $200,000. Radcliffe expects to realize the outside basis difference through future dividends, which qualify for a 65% dividends received deduction. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What deferred tax liability should Radcliffe recognize on the current year's undistributed earnings?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0, because the earnings are undistributed

questions.explanation: Because realization is expected through dividends, the deferred tax liability is measured on the portion that will actually be taxable after the dividends received deduction: $200,000 × (1 − 65%) = $70,000 taxable, × 25% = $17,500. $50,000 ignores the DRD; $32,500 taxes the deductible portion instead of the taxable portion; the indefinite reversal exception does not apply to a domestic equity method investee that is not a subsidiary, so $0 is incorrect.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-30-25-5以下により、持分法投資（50%以下保有の国内投資先）の未分配利益には原則DTLを認識する。配当による実現を見込む場合、受取配当益金不算入（DRD）65%考慮後の課税部分で測定する：$200,000 × 35% = $70,000、DTL = $70,000 × 25% = $17,500。APB 23の無期限再投資例外は国内の持分法投資先には適用されない。

questions.stem: Sheffield Corp. has historically asserted that the $2,000,000 of undistributed earnings of its foreign subsidiary are indefinitely reinvested and has recognized no related deferred taxes. In the current year, management changes its plans and now intends to repatriate all of those earnings. Upon distribution, the only tax cost will be a 5% foreign withholding tax; no residual home-country tax will be due. What should Sheffield record in the period the assertion changes?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Nothing until the dividend is actually declared

choices.label: B
choices.text: A deferred tax liability of $420,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: A deferred tax liability of $520,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: A deferred tax liability of $100,000

questions.explanation: When an entity can no longer assert indefinite reinvestment, it must recognize a deferred tax liability for the tax expected on remittance: $2,000,000 × 5% withholding = $100,000, recognized in the period the assertion changes, not when the dividend is declared. $420,000 incorrectly applies a 21% rate and $520,000 adds withholding on top of a residual tax that the facts state will not be due.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-30-25-19の無期限再投資の例外（APB 23例外）は、再投資の意思を主張できなくなった時点で適用を停止し、送金時に見込まれる税金についてDTLを認識する。本問では送金時のコストは外国源泉税5%のみ：DTL = $2,000,000 × 5% = $100,000。認識時点は意思決定（主張の変更）が行われた期であり、配当宣言時ではない。

questions.stem: Thackeray Corp. collects $480,000 of three-year subscription fees on January 1, Year 1. For tax purposes the entire amount is taxable upon receipt. For book purposes, revenue is recognized evenly over three years, so $160,000 is recognized in Year 1 and $320,000 remains in deferred revenue at December 31, Year 1. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What deferred tax amount should Thackeray report at December 31, Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A deferred tax liability of $80,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: A deferred tax asset of $120,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: A deferred tax asset of $80,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: A deferred tax asset of $40,000

questions.explanation: The deferred revenue liability has a book basis of $320,000 and a tax basis of zero (the income was already taxed). The future book revenue recognition will not be taxed again, creating a deductible temporary difference of $320,000 and a deferred tax asset of $320,000 × 25% = $80,000. A liability is the wrong direction because tax income preceded book income; $120,000 uses the full $480,000 receipt; $40,000 uses only one remaining year ($160,000).
questions.explanationJa: 前受収益は税務上受領時に全額課税済みのため、帳簿上の負債$320,000に対し税務基準額はゼロ。将来、帳簿上収益認識される際に再課税されないため将来減算一時差異となり、DTA = $320,000 × 25% = $80,000（ASC 740-10-25-20）。課税が帳簿認識より先行しているのでDTLではなくDTAである。$120,000は受領総額$480,000を使った誤り。

questions.stem: Umberton Mining Co. reports pretax book income of $1,000,000 for Year 1, which reflects cost depletion of $100,000. For tax purposes, Umberton deducts percentage (statutory) depletion of $160,000. The $60,000 excess of percentage depletion over cost depletion will never reverse. The enacted tax rate is 25%, and there are no other differences. What is Umberton's total income tax expense for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Percentage depletion in excess of cost depletion is a permanent difference, so it reduces both taxable income and total tax expense with no deferred tax consequence. Total tax expense = ($1,000,000 − $60,000) × 25% = $235,000, which here equals the current tax payable. $250,000 ignores the permanent difference; $265,000 adds it instead of subtracting; $240,000 reflects an arbitrary partial adjustment.
questions.explanationJa: コスト基準を超える法定（百分率）減耗控除は将来戻り入れられることのない永久差異であり、繰延税金を生じさせず実効税率を法定税率より引き下げる。総税金費用 = ($1,000,000 − $60,000) × 25% = $235,000。永久差異は当期税金と総税金費用の双方を減少させる点で一時差異と異なる（ASC 740-10-25-30参照）。

questions.subtopic: DTL on Partially Collected Installment Sale
questions.stem: Vanderhoff Corp. sells land in Year 1 and recognizes the entire $300,000 gain for financial reporting purposes. For tax purposes, the gain is reported under the installment method as cash is collected: $100,000 of the gain is taxed in Year 1 and the remaining $200,000 will be taxed in future years. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What deferred tax liability should Vanderhoff report at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Only the portion of the gain not yet taxed creates a temporary difference at year-end: $200,000 × 25% = $50,000 deferred tax liability. $75,000 applies the rate to the entire gain, ignoring the $100,000 already taxed currently; $25,000 applies the rate to the taxed portion; $0 ignores the remaining difference.
questions.explanationJa: 割賦基準により税務上の認識が繰延べられる利得は将来加算一時差異を生む（ASC 740-10-25-20）。期末時点で未課税の部分のみが一時差異となるため、DTL = ($300,000 − $100,000) × 25% = $50,000。$75,000は当期に課税済みの$100,000を含めて計算した誤り。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Benefit from Net DTA Rollforward
questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 2, Wexford Corp. reported a gross deferred tax asset of $250,000 and a valuation allowance of $100,000. At the end of Year 2, the gross deferred tax asset is $320,000 and the required valuation allowance is $60,000. Wexford has no deferred tax liabilities. What deferred income tax benefit should Wexford report in Year 2?
questions.explanation: Deferred tax expense or benefit equals the change in the net deferred tax position. Net DTA moved from $250,000 − $100,000 = $150,000 to $320,000 − $60,000 = $260,000, an increase of $110,000, which is a deferred tax benefit. $70,000 is only the change in the gross asset; $40,000 is only the change in the valuation allowance; $30,000 incorrectly nets the two changes against each other.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金費用（便益）は正味繰延税金ポジションの期中変動額で測定する（ASC 740-10-45-20参照）。正味DTAは期首 $250,000 − $100,000 = $150,000 から期末 $320,000 − $60,000 = $260,000 へ$110,000増加しており、これが繰延税金便益となる。グロスDTAの増加$70,000と評価性引当金の減少$40,000の両方が便益方向に働く点に注意（70,000 + 40,000 = 110,000）。

questions.stem: Yardley Corp. has income from continuing operations before tax of $600,000 and a gain from discontinued operations before tax of $150,000. The enacted tax rate applicable to all items is 25%. At what amount should the discontinued operations be presented in the income statement?
questions.explanation: Under intraperiod tax allocation, discontinued operations are presented net of their directly allocable tax: $150,000 − ($150,000 × 25%) = $112,500. $150,000 is the pretax gain; $37,500 is the tax alone; $120,000 incorrectly applies a 20% rate.
questions.explanationJa: 期間内税金配分（ASC 740-20-45）により、非継続事業は関連する税金を控除した純額で表示する。非継続事業の税金 = $150,000 × 25% = $37,500、表示額 = $150,000 − $37,500 = $112,500。継続事業の税金費用$150,000（$600,000 × 25%）とは区分して配分・表示する。

questions.subtopic: Measurement Period Adjustment to Acquired Valuation Allowance
questions.stem: Six months after acquiring Target Co. in a business combination, Acquirer Corp. obtains new information about facts and circumstances that existed as of the acquisition date, leading it to reduce the valuation allowance on Target's acquired deferred tax assets by $120,000. The measurement period has not yet ended. How should Acquirer record this adjustment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a $120,000 reduction of income tax expense in the period of the change

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a $120,000 credit to additional paid-in capital

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a $120,000 credit to retained earnings as a prior period adjustment

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a $120,000 reduction of goodwill

questions.explanation: During the measurement period, adjustments to acquired deferred tax balances that result from new information about facts existing at the acquisition date are recognized as adjustments to goodwill (first reducing goodwill, then bargain purchase gain). Recognizing the change in income tax expense is correct only after the measurement period ends or when the change results from post-acquisition events; equity accounts are never the destination for this adjustment.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-740-45-2により、測定期間（最長1年）内に取得日時点で存在した事実に関する新情報により取得したDTAの評価性引当金を修正する場合は、のれんの修正として処理する（のれんをゼロまで減額後は割安購入益）。測定期間経過後の変更や取得後の事象に起因する変更は税金費用で認識する。本問は測定期間内かつ取得日時点の事実に関する情報であるため、のれんの減額$120,000となる。

questions.subtopic: Tax Allocation in Separate Subsidiary Financial Statements
questions.stem: Subsidiary S is a member of a consolidated group that files a single consolidated tax return. S issues its own separate financial statements. Under ASC 740, which method of allocating consolidated income tax expense to S is acceptable for S's separate financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Allocating tax expense in proportion to each member's share of consolidated revenue

choices.label: B
choices.text: A method that is systematic, rational, and consistent with the broad principles of ASC 740, such as the separate return method

choices.label: C
choices.text: No allocation is permitted; only the parent may report income tax expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: Allocating only the amount of cash actually paid to the parent under the tax sharing agreement, regardless of method

questions.explanation: ASC 740-10-30-27 requires that the consolidated tax provision be allocated to members issuing separate statements using a method that is systematic, rational, and consistent with the broad principles of ASC 740; the separate return method meets those criteria. Revenue-proportional allocation is not consistent with ASC 740's asset-and-liability principles; members must present their allocated tax expense; amounts paid under a tax sharing agreement may differ from an acceptable allocation, with the difference treated as a capital transaction.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-27により、連結納税グループの構成会社が単体財務諸表を作成する場合、連結税金費用を「体系的・合理的でASC 740の基本原則に整合する方法」で配分しなければならず、セパレートリターン法（単独申告を仮定する方法）はこれを満たす代表的な方法である。収益比例配分は資産負債法の原則に整合しない。タックスシェアリング契約上の授受額と配分額の差は資本取引として扱われる。

questions.stem: Which of the following taxes is within the scope of ASC 740, Income Taxes?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Sales taxes collected from customers and remitted to the state

choices.label: B
choices.text: Property taxes assessed on the fair value of real estate holdings

choices.label: C
choices.text: A state franchise tax to the extent it is based on the entity's income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Employer payroll taxes on employee wages

questions.explanation: ASC 740 applies to taxes based on income. A franchise tax is within its scope to the extent the tax is computed on income (any portion based on capital or another base is accounted for outside ASC 740 as an operating expense). Sales taxes, property taxes, and payroll taxes are not based on income and are excluded from ASC 740.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740の適用範囲は「所得（income）に基づく税金」である。フランチャイズ税は所得を課税標準とする部分についてASC 740の対象となり、資本等を課税標準とする部分は営業費用として処理する（ASC 740-10-15-4）。売上税・固定資産税・給与税は所得課税ではないため対象外。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2023-09, Improvements to Income Tax Disclosures, which of the following describes the enhanced rate reconciliation disclosure required for public business entities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A qualitative description of rate reconciliation items, with no numerical detail required

choices.label: B
choices.text: Disclosure of only the total difference between the statutory rate and the effective rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: A reconciliation limited to federal items, with state and foreign effects combined into a single line

choices.label: D
choices.text: A tabular reconciliation using specified categories (such as state and local taxes, foreign tax effects, and tax credits), with separate disclosure of items equal to or greater than 5% of the amount computed at the statutory rate

questions.explanation: ASU 2023-09 requires public business entities to present the rate reconciliation in a tabular format using eight specified categories, with separate disclosure (and disaggregation by nature and, for some categories, jurisdiction) of any reconciling item equal to or greater than 5% of the amount computed by multiplying pretax income by the statutory rate. A qualitative-only description is permitted for entities other than public business entities, not for PBEs; total-only and federal-only presentations do not satisfy the disaggregation requirements.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2023-09（ASC 740-10-50-12A）は、公開営利企業（PBE）に対し、州・地方税、外国税の影響、税額控除等の特定カテゴリーによる表形式の税率調整表を要求し、法定税率×税引前利益の5%以上の調整項目は個別開示（性質別・一部は法域別に細分化）を求める。定性的説明のみで足りるのは非PBEであり、PBEには認められない。年次期間に適用される。

questions.stem: Under ASC 740-10-50, how do the income tax rate reconciliation disclosure requirements for entities other than public business entities differ from those for public business entities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Nonpublic entities must provide the same numerical reconciliation but may omit the statutory rate used

choices.label: B
choices.text: Nonpublic entities may disclose the nature of significant reconciling items qualitatively and are not required to present a numerical reconciliation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Nonpublic entities are exempt from all income tax disclosures

choices.label: D
choices.text: Nonpublic entities must present the reconciliation in dollars but never in percentages

questions.explanation: Entities other than public business entities are not required to present the numerical rate reconciliation; instead, they must disclose the nature of significant reconciling items between the statutory rate and the effective rate qualitatively. They are not exempt from other income tax disclosures (such as the components of deferred tax assets and liabilities), and public entities may present the reconciliation in either percentages or dollar amounts.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-50-13により、非公開企業（PBE以外）は数値による税率調整表の開示を要求されず、法定税率と実効税率の重要な差異項目の内容を定性的に開示すればよい。ただし繰延税金の主要な内訳等、他の税金開示が免除されるわけではない。PBEは調整表を金額または比率のいずれでも表示できる（ASC 740-10-50-12）。

questions.stem: A change in the enacted tax rate causes deferred taxes originally recorded through other comprehensive income to be remeasured through income tax expense from continuing operations, leaving disproportionate ("stranded") tax effects in accumulated other comprehensive income. Under ASU 2018-02, how may an entity address stranded tax effects resulting from the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Elect to reclassify the stranded tax effects from accumulated other comprehensive income to retained earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reverse the rate-change remeasurement out of continuing operations and record it in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: Amortize the stranded tax effects into income tax expense over the average reversal period

choices.label: D
choices.text: Write off the stranded tax effects directly to income from continuing operations

questions.explanation: ASU 2018-02 permits (but does not require) an election to reclassify the stranded tax effects of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act from accumulated other comprehensive income to retained earnings, an equity-to-equity transfer. Recording the rate-change remeasurement in OCI would violate the prohibition on backwards tracing; amortization into expense and direct write-off to continuing operations are not provided for in the guidance.
questions.explanationJa: 米国GAAPはbackwards tracing（税率変更等の影響を当初計上した区分に遡って計上すること）を禁止しているため、税率変更時にOCI起源の繰延税金の再測定差額は継続事業の税金費用に計上され、AOCIに「取り残された（stranded）」税効果が残る。ASU 2018-02（ASC 220-10-45-12B）は、TCJAに起因するstranded tax effectsについて、AOCIから利益剰余金への振替（資本内の振替）を選択適用として認めた。OCIで再測定する処理はbackwards tracing禁止に反する。

questions.subtopic: DTA Measured at Scheduled Future Enacted Rates
questions.stem: Zelinski Corp. has a deferred compensation liability of $500,000 at December 31, Year 1, deductible for tax purposes when paid. Of this amount, $200,000 will be paid in Year 2 and $300,000 will be paid in Year 3 and later. Enacted tax law sets the rate at 25% for Year 2 and 22% for Year 3 and all later years. What deferred tax asset should Zelinski report at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Deferred taxes are measured at the enacted rates expected to apply in the periods the differences reverse: ($200,000 × 25%) + ($300,000 × 22%) = $50,000 + $66,000 = $116,000. $125,000 applies 25% to the entire difference; $110,000 applies 22% to the entire difference; $119,000 reverses the scheduling, applying 25% to the Year 3 portion and 22% to the Year 2 portion.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-8により、繰延税金は一時差異が解消する期に適用される制定済み税率で測定する。解消スケジュールに応じて、Year 2解消分 $200,000 × 25% = $50,000、Year 3以降解消分 $300,000 × 22% = $66,000、合計DTA = $116,000。全額に単一税率を適用する計算（$125,000や$110,000）は、段階的税率変更が制定されている場合には誤りとなる。

questions.stem: In Year 1, Abernathy Corp. incurs $600,000 of domestic research and experimental (R&E) expenditures, all expensed immediately for financial reporting. Under IRC Section 174 (as amended for tax years beginning after 2021), the costs must be capitalized for tax purposes and amortized over 5 years using a half-year convention, producing a Year 1 tax deduction of $60,000. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What deferred tax asset arises from this item at the end of Year 1?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0, because R&E costs are a permanent difference

questions.explanation: The tax basis of the capitalized R&E is $600,000 − $60,000 = $540,000, while the book basis is zero, creating a deductible temporary difference of $540,000 and a deferred tax asset of $540,000 × 25% = $135,000. $150,000 ignores the Year 1 tax amortization; $120,000 assumes a full year of amortization ($120,000 deducted); the difference fully reverses through future tax amortization, so it is temporary, not permanent.
questions.explanationJa: 2022年以後開始事業年度より、IRC Section 174は国内R&E費用の税務上の資産計上と5年償却（半年規則）を要求する。帳簿上は即時費用化（基準額ゼロ）に対し、税務基準額 = $600,000 − 初年度償却$60,000 = $540,000。将来減算一時差異$540,000 × 25% = DTA $135,000。将来の税務償却により全額解消されるため一時差異であり、永久差異ではない。

questions.subtopic: NOL Carryback Refund at Differing Prior-Year Rates
questions.stem: Under a special statutory provision, Bancroft Corp. may carry back its Year 3 net operating loss of $350,000 to the two preceding years, applying the loss to the earliest year first. Year 1 taxable income was $200,000, taxed at an enacted rate of 30%; Year 2 taxable income was $150,000, taxed at an enacted rate of 25%. What income tax refund receivable should Bancroft recognize in Year 3?
questions.explanation: The carryback benefit is measured using the tax rates of the years to which the loss is carried: $200,000 × 30% = $60,000 from Year 1, plus the remaining $150,000 × 25% = $37,500 from Year 2, for a total refund of $97,500. $87,500 applies 25% to the entire loss; $105,000 applies 30% to the entire loss; $73,500 incorrectly uses a 21% rate.
questions.explanationJa: 欠損金の繰戻し還付は、繰り戻す各年度に実際に適用された税率で測定し、税金還付債権（受取債権）と当期の税務便益を認識する。Year 1充当分 $200,000 × 30% = $60,000、残額のYear 2充当分 $150,000 × 25% = $37,500、還付合計 = $97,500。繰戻しは当期の制定税率ではなく過年度の納付実績に基づく点が繰越し（将来税率で測定）と異なる。

questions.subtopic: DTA from Book Impairment of Equipment
questions.stem: Caldwell Corp. recorded an impairment loss on equipment for financial reporting purposes that is not deductible for tax purposes until the asset is sold or disposed of. At year-end, the equipment's book carrying amount is $420,000 and its tax basis is $470,000. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What deferred tax amount should Caldwell report related to this equipment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A deferred tax liability of $12,500

choices.label: B
choices.text: A deferred tax asset of $50,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: A deferred tax asset of $12,500

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0, because the deduction depends on a future sale

questions.explanation: The tax basis ($470,000) exceeds the book carrying amount ($420,000) by $50,000, a deductible temporary difference: when the asset is recovered, tax deductions will exceed book expense by $50,000. The deferred tax asset is $50,000 × 25% = $12,500. A liability arises only when book basis exceeds tax basis; $50,000 is the gross difference; the dependence on future recovery does not prevent recognition, although realizability is assessed through the valuation allowance.
questions.explanationJa: 資産負債法（ASC 740-10-30-5）では帳簿価額と税務基準額の差異で繰延税金を測定する。税務基準額$470,000 > 帳簿価額$420,000のため将来減算一時差異$50,000が存在し、DTA = $50,000 × 25% = $12,500。帳簿上の減損は税務上売却等まで損金算入されないため、回収時に税務上の控除が帳簿費用を上回る。実現可能性は評価性引当金で別途検討する。

questions.subtopic: Interim Treatment of Valuation Allowance Changes
questions.stem: During the second quarter, Dalton Corp. changes its judgment about the realizability of a deferred tax asset that existed at the beginning of the year. Under ASC 740-270, which statement correctly describes the interim accounting for changes in a beginning-of-year valuation allowance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All changes in the valuation allowance are always treated as discrete items in the quarter of the change

choices.label: B
choices.text: A change resulting from expected current-year ordinary income is included in the estimated annual effective tax rate, while a change in judgment about realization in future years is recognized discretely in the quarter of the change

choices.label: C
choices.text: All changes in the valuation allowance are spread evenly over the remaining quarters of the year

choices.label: D
choices.text: Changes in the valuation allowance may not be recognized until the annual financial statements are prepared

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-270, a release or increase of the beginning-of-year valuation allowance that is attributable to income expected to be earned in the current year is built into the estimated annual effective tax rate, whereas a change driven by revised expectations about future years' income is a discrete event recognized entirely in the interim period in which the judgment changes. Treating every change as discrete, spreading changes evenly, or deferring recognition to year-end all misstate the model.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-270-25-4以下により、期首評価性引当金の変動のうち、当年度のordinary income（経常的な当年度所得）による実現見込みに起因する部分は見積年間実効税率（AETR）に織り込み、将来年度の所得見通しの判断変更に起因する部分は判断変更が生じた四半期に離散項目（discrete item）として全額認識する。すべてを離散処理する、残存四半期に均等配分する、年度末まで認識しないといった処理はいずれも誤り。

questions.stem: All of the following are permanent differences between pretax financial income and taxable income EXCEPT:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Warranty costs accrued for book purposes but deductible for tax purposes when paid

choices.label: B
choices.text: Interest income on municipal bonds

choices.label: C
choices.text: Fines and penalties paid for violations of law

choices.label: D
choices.text: The dividends received deduction on qualifying dividends from domestic corporations

questions.explanation: Accrued warranty costs reverse when the warranty claims are paid and deducted, making them a deductible temporary difference that creates a deferred tax asset. Municipal bond interest is never taxable, fines and penalties are never deductible, and the dividends received deduction is never reversed, so each of those is a permanent difference with no deferred tax consequence.
questions.explanationJa: 永久差異は将来解消されることがなく繰延税金を生じさせない。地方債利息（益金不算入）、罰科金（損金不算入）、受取配当益金不算入（DRD）はいずれも永久差異。一方、製品保証引当金は帳簿上の費用認識が先行し、税務上は支払時に損金算入されるため、将来解消する将来減算一時差異であり、DTAを生じさせる（ASC 740-10-25-20）。

questions.subtopic: Total Tax Expense with Permanent and Temporary Differences
questions.stem: Eastman Corp. reports pretax financial income of $2,400,000 for Year 1, which includes $100,000 of nontaxable municipal bond interest. Tax depreciation exceeds book depreciation by $300,000 (a temporary difference). The enacted tax rate is 25% for all years. What is Eastman's total income tax expense for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Total tax expense equals the rate applied to book income adjusted only for permanent differences: ($2,400,000 − $100,000) × 25% = $575,000. As proof: taxable income = $2,400,000 − $100,000 − $300,000 = $2,000,000, current tax = $500,000, deferred tax expense = $300,000 × 25% = $75,000, total = $575,000. $600,000 ignores the permanent difference; $500,000 is the current portion only; $525,000 incorrectly treats the temporary difference as permanent.

questions.subtopic: Deferred Portion of the Tax Provision
questions.stem: During Year 1, Fairbanks Corp.'s deferred tax liabilities increased by $40,000 and its deferred tax assets increased by $15,000. Current income tax payable for the year is $180,000. What is the deferred portion of Fairbanks's income tax provision for Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $55,000 expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: $25,000 benefit

choices.label: C
choices.text: $25,000 expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: $205,000 expense

questions.explanation: Deferred tax expense equals the increase in deferred tax liabilities minus the increase in deferred tax assets: $40,000 − $15,000 = $25,000 expense. $55,000 incorrectly adds the two changes; a benefit would arise only if the net deferred position improved; $205,000 combines the current payable with the deferred amount, which is the total provision rather than the deferred portion.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金費用は正味繰延税金ポジションの変動で測定する：DTL増加$40,000 − DTA増加$15,000 = $25,000の費用。DTAの増加は税金費用を減らす方向に働くため、両者を加算（$55,000）するのは誤り。なお総税金費用は当期分$180,000 + 繰延分$25,000 = $205,000となるが、問われているのは繰延部分である。

questions.subtopic: Classifying Temporary Differences as DTA or DTL
questions.stem: At year-end, Brockton Corp. has two temporary differences: (1) tax depreciation taken to date exceeds book depreciation, and (2) a warranty liability is accrued for book purposes but is deductible for tax only when paid. How should each difference be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Depreciation: deferred tax asset; Warranty: deferred tax asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: Depreciation: deferred tax asset; Warranty: deferred tax liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: Depreciation: deferred tax liability; Warranty: deferred tax asset

choices.label: D
choices.text: Depreciation: deferred tax liability; Warranty: deferred tax liability

questions.explanation: Excess tax depreciation means the asset's tax basis is below its book carrying amount, so future book depreciation will exceed future tax deductions, producing future taxable amounts — a deferred tax liability. The accrued warranty liability has been expensed for book purposes but will be deducted for tax in the future when paid, producing future deductible amounts — a deferred tax asset. Classifying both items the same way, or reversing them, confuses which differences generate future taxable amounts versus future deductible amounts.
questions.explanationJa: 税務上の減価償却が会計上を上回っている場合、資産の税務基準額は帳簿価額を下回っており、将来は会計上の償却費が税務上の損金を上回るため、将来課税額（future taxable amounts）が生じる＝繰延税金負債（DTL）。一方、製品保証引当金は会計上は費用計上済みだが、税務上は支払時に損金算入されるため、将来減算額（future deductible amounts）が生じる＝繰延税金資産（DTA）。両者を同じ区分にしたり逆にしたりするのは、将来課税額と将来減算額の区別を誤った考え方である。

questions.subtopic: Current Tax Payable with Permanent and Temporary Items
questions.stem: Whitman Corp. reports pretax financial income of $400,000 for Year 1, which includes $25,000 of interest income on municipal bonds. Tax depreciation exceeds book depreciation by $60,000. The enacted tax rate is 21%. What is Whitman's current income tax payable for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Taxable income = $400,000 − $25,000 municipal interest (permanently nontaxable) − $60,000 excess tax depreciation (temporary difference) = $315,000. Current tax payable = $315,000 × 21% = $66,150. The amount $84,000 applies the rate to book income without any adjustment; $78,750 removes only the municipal interest; $71,400 removes only the depreciation difference.
questions.explanationJa: 課税所得 = 400,000 − 25,000（地方債利息、永久に非課税）− 60,000（税務上の超過償却、一時差異）= 315,000ドル。当期未払税金 = 315,000 × 21% = 66,150ドル。84,000ドルは調整なしで会計上の利益に税率を乗じた誤り、78,750ドルは地方債利息のみを控除した誤り、71,400ドルは減価償却差異のみを控除した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Balance Sheet Presentation - Single Jurisdiction Netting
questions.stem: Stanton Corp. operates in a single tax jurisdiction. At year-end it has a gross deferred tax asset of $200,000, a related valuation allowance of $50,000, and a deferred tax liability of $90,000. Under ASU 2015-17, how should Stanton present deferred taxes in its classified balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A single noncurrent deferred tax asset of $60,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: A noncurrent deferred tax asset of $150,000 and a noncurrent deferred tax liability of $90,000, shown separately

choices.label: C
choices.text: A current deferred tax asset of $60,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: A single noncurrent deferred tax asset of $110,000

questions.explanation: All deferred tax assets and liabilities are classified as noncurrent, and amounts within the same tax jurisdiction are offset and presented as a single net amount. Net deferred tax asset = $200,000 − $50,000 valuation allowance − $90,000 DTL = $60,000 noncurrent asset. Presenting the asset and liability separately is not permitted within one jurisdiction; a current classification no longer exists for deferred taxes; and $110,000 ignores the valuation allowance.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金資産・負債はすべて非流動に分類し、同一税務管轄区域内では相殺して純額で表示する。純額の繰延税金資産 = 200,000 − 評価性引当金50,000 − DTL 90,000 = 60,000ドル（非流動資産）。同一管轄区域内で資産と負債を別建て表示することは認められず、繰延税金の流動区分は現行基準では存在しない。110,000ドルは評価性引当金を無視した誤りである。

questions.stem: In evaluating whether a tax position meets the more-likely-than-not recognition threshold under ASC 740-10, an entity must presume which of the following?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The position will not be examined because the entity has never been audited

choices.label: B
choices.text: The taxing authority will settle the position through negotiation at 50% of the benefit

choices.label: C
choices.text: Detection risk may be considered if the position is immaterial

choices.label: D
choices.text: The position will be examined by a taxing authority that has full knowledge of all relevant information

questions.explanation: ASC 740-10-25-6 requires the entity to assume the position will be examined by the relevant taxing authority having full knowledge of all relevant information. The recognition assessment is based solely on the technical merits of the position. Past audit history and detection risk (the chance the authority never finds the position) may not be considered, and an assumed negotiated settlement percentage relates to measurement-style thinking, not the recognition threshold.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-6 は、税務ポジションが「すべての関連情報を完全に把握した税務当局によって調査される」と仮定して more-likely-than-not（50%超）の認識基準を判定することを要求している。認識の判定はポジションの技術的根拠（technical merits）のみに基づく。過去に税務調査を受けていないことや発見リスク（当局に発見されない可能性）を考慮することは認められず、「50%で交渉により和解する」という仮定は認識基準の判定とは無関係である。

questions.subtopic: Permanent Differences - Effect on Effective Tax Rate
questions.stem: Which of the following permanent differences would cause a company's effective tax rate to be HIGHER than the statutory tax rate?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Interest income on municipal bonds

choices.label: B
choices.text: The dividends received deduction

choices.label: C
choices.text: An impairment of nondeductible goodwill

choices.label: D
choices.text: Percentage depletion in excess of cost depletion

questions.explanation: An impairment loss on goodwill that is not deductible for tax reduces book income without reducing taxable income, so total tax expense is spread over a smaller book income base, pushing the effective rate above the statutory rate. Municipal bond interest, the dividends received deduction, and excess percentage depletion are all items included in book income (or deductions allowed for tax beyond book expense) that are never taxed, which lowers the effective rate below the statutory rate.
questions.explanationJa: 税務上損金算入できないのれんの減損は、会計上の利益を減らすが課税所得は減らさないため、税金費用がより小さい会計上の利益に対して計算される形になり、実効税率は法定税率より高くなる。地方債利息、受取配当控除（DRD）、コスト法を超える割合減耗は、いずれも会計上の利益に含まれる（または税務上のみ認められる控除である）が課税されない項目であり、実効税率を法定税率より低くする方向に働く。

questions.stem: In Year 1, Granger Corp. incurs a federal net operating loss of $900,000 (generated after 2017, carried forward indefinitely under current law). The enacted tax rate is 21%, and no valuation allowance is needed. What deferred tax asset should Granger recognize for the NOL carryforward at the end of Year 1?
questions.explanation: The deferred tax asset equals the full NOL carryforward times the enacted rate: $900,000 × 21% = $189,000. The 80%-of-taxable-income limitation restricts how much NOL can be used in any single future year, but it does not reduce the amount of the carryforward itself, so the gross DTA is not measured at 80% ($151,200 reflects that error). Using 30% ($270,000) applies a rate that is not the enacted rate, and recognizing nothing ignores that an NOL carryforward is a deferred tax asset.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金資産は NOL繰越額の全額に法定税率を乗じて算定する：900,000 × 21% = 189,000ドル。課税所得の80%という利用制限は、将来の各年度に使用できるNOLの額を制限するものであって、繰越額そのものを減額するものではないため、グロスのDTAを80%で測定する（151,200ドル）のは誤り。270,000ドルは法定外の30%を使った誤りで、ゼロとするのはNOL繰越が繰延税金資産を生むことを無視している。

questions.subtopic: Life Insurance Permanent Differences - Current Tax
questions.stem: Pratt Corp. reports pretax financial income of $750,000 for Year 1. Included in this amount are $100,000 of proceeds received on a key-officer life insurance policy (Pratt is the beneficiary) and $20,000 of premiums paid on the same policy, which were expensed for book purposes. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What is Pratt's current income tax payable for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Life insurance proceeds where the company is beneficiary are nontaxable (subtract $100,000), and the related premiums are nondeductible (add back $20,000). Both are permanent differences. Taxable income = $750,000 − $100,000 + $20,000 = $670,000; current tax payable = $670,000 × 25% = $167,500. The amount $187,500 makes no adjustments; $162,500 removes the proceeds but forgets to add back the premiums; $207,500 reverses the direction of both adjustments.
questions.explanationJa: 会社が受取人である生命保険金は非課税（100,000ドルを減算）、対応する保険料は損金不算入（20,000ドルを加算）。いずれも永久差異である。課税所得 = 750,000 − 100,000 + 20,000 = 670,000ドル、当期未払税金 = 670,000 × 25% = 167,500ドル。187,500ドルは無調整、162,500ドルは保険金の減算のみで保険料の加算を忘れたもの、207,500ドルは両調整の符号を逆にした誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Current Tax with Originating and Reversing Differences
questions.stem: Mercer Corp. reports pretax financial income of $1,000,000 for Year 2. During the year, tax depreciation exceeded book depreciation by $180,000 (an originating taxable difference). Warranty expense accrued for book purposes was $90,000, while warranty claims actually paid (deductible for tax) were $60,000. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What is Mercer's current income tax payable for Year 2?
questions.explanation: Taxable income = $1,000,000 − $180,000 excess tax depreciation + $30,000 warranty adjustment (book expense $90,000 exceeds tax deduction $60,000) = $850,000. Current tax payable = $850,000 × 25% = $212,500. The amount $250,000 ignores both differences; $205,000 adjusts only for depreciation and omits the warranty add-back; $257,500 omits the depreciation adjustment entirely while correctly adding back the warranty ($1,000,000 + $30,000 = $1,030,000; $1,030,000 × 25% = $257,500).
questions.explanationJa: 課税所得 = 1,000,000 − 180,000（税務上の超過償却）+ 30,000（製品保証：会計上の費用90,000 − 税務上の損金60,000）= 850,000ドル。当期未払税金 = 850,000 × 25% = 212,500ドル。250,000ドルは両差異とも無視した誤り、205,000ドルは減価償却のみ調整し製品保証の加算を漏らした誤り、257,500ドルは製品保証の加算は正しく行ったものの減価償却差異の調整を完全に省略した誤り（1,000,000 + 30,000 = 1,030,000 × 25% = 257,500）である。

questions.subtopic: Net Deferred Tax from Two Temporary Differences
questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Talbot Corp. has two temporary differences: an accrued litigation liability of $300,000 that will be deductible for tax when paid, and prepaid rent of $120,000 that was deducted for tax when paid but will be expensed for book purposes next year. The enacted tax rate is 25%. What net deferred tax amount should Talbot report at year-end?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net deferred tax asset of $105,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net deferred tax asset of $75,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net deferred tax asset of $45,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net deferred tax liability of $30,000

questions.explanation: The litigation accrual creates a deductible temporary difference: DTA = $300,000 × 25% = $75,000. The prepaid rent already deducted for tax creates a taxable temporary difference (book expense comes later with no further tax deduction): DTL = $120,000 × 25% = $30,000. Net deferred tax asset = $75,000 − $30,000 = $45,000. Adding the two to get $105,000 treats both as assets; $75,000 alone ignores the prepaid rent; a net liability of $30,000 ignores the litigation accrual.
questions.explanationJa: 訴訟引当金は将来減算一時差異を生む：DTA = 300,000 × 25% = 75,000ドル。税務上すでに損金算入された前払家賃は将来加算一時差異を生む（会計上の費用化時に追加の損金算入はない）：DTL = 120,000 × 25% = 30,000ドル。純額の繰延税金資産 = 75,000 − 30,000 = 45,000ドル。両者を合算した105,000ドルは双方を資産扱いした誤り、75,000ドルは前払家賃の無視、30,000ドルの負債は訴訟引当金の無視である。

questions.subtopic: Rate Change with Both DTA and DTL Outstanding
questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 3, Voss Corp. has a deferred tax liability of $300,000 (related to a $1,000,000 taxable temporary difference) and a deferred tax asset of $120,000 (related to a $400,000 deductible temporary difference), both measured at the 30% enacted rate. During Year 3, a new tax rate of 25% is enacted, effective immediately. What is the effect of the rate change on Year 3 income tax expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $50,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease of $30,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase of $20,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease of $70,000

questions.explanation: Remeasure both balances at 25%: the DTL falls from $300,000 to $250,000, a $50,000 benefit; the DTA falls from $120,000 to $100,000, a $20,000 expense. The net effect, recorded in income tax expense from continuing operations in the period of enactment, is a $30,000 decrease ($50,000 benefit − $20,000 expense). A $50,000 decrease considers only the liability; a $20,000 increase considers only the asset; a $70,000 decrease incorrectly adds the two effects in the same direction.
questions.explanationJa: 両残高を25%で再測定する：DTLは300,000→250,000ドルで50,000ドルの税金費用減少（ベネフィット）、DTAは120,000→100,000ドルで20,000ドルの税金費用増加。純額の影響は、制定された期の継続事業の税金費用において30,000ドルの減少（50,000の減少 − 20,000の増加）。50,000ドルの減少は負債側のみ、20,000ドルの増加は資産側のみを考慮した誤りで、70,000ドルの減少は両効果を同方向に加算した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Valuation Allowance Limited by Projected Taxable Income
questions.stem: Corwin Corp. has a deductible temporary difference of $800,000 that will reverse evenly over the next several years. The enacted tax rate is 30%, so the gross deferred tax asset is $240,000. Based on all available evidence, Corwin projects that cumulative future taxable income available to absorb the reversals will be only $500,000, and no other sources of realization (carrybacks, reversing DTLs, or tax-planning strategies) exist. What valuation allowance should Corwin report?
questions.explanation: Only $500,000 of the $800,000 deductible difference is more likely than not to be realized against future taxable income. Realizable DTA = $500,000 × 30% = $150,000, so the valuation allowance = $240,000 gross DTA − $150,000 realizable = $90,000. Reporting no allowance ignores the income limitation; a full $240,000 allowance ignores the income that is available; $150,000 is the realizable portion of the asset, not the allowance.
questions.explanationJa: 将来減算一時差異800,000ドルのうち、将来の課税所得で実現が見込めるのは500,000ドルのみ。実現可能なDTA = 500,000 × 30% = 150,000ドル。よって評価性引当金 = グロスDTA 240,000 − 実現可能額150,000 = 90,000ドル。引当金ゼロは課税所得の制約を無視、全額240,000ドルは利用可能な所得を無視した誤り。150,000ドルは「実現可能なDTAの額」であって引当金の額ではない。

questions.subtopic: Change in Valuation Allowance - Income Statement Effect
questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 4, Halloran Corp. had a valuation allowance of $220,000 against its deferred tax assets. At the end of Year 4, the gross deferred tax asset is $600,000, and management concludes that it is more likely than not that 75% of the asset will be realized. What is the effect of the change in the valuation allowance on Year 4 income tax expense from continuing operations?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $70,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase of $150,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase of $70,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No effect; the change is recorded in other comprehensive income

questions.explanation: The required ending allowance equals the portion not expected to be realized: $600,000 × 25% = $150,000. The allowance decreases from $220,000 to $150,000, a $70,000 reduction that is credited to income tax expense — a $70,000 decrease in expense. An increase of $150,000 confuses the ending balance with the change; an increase of $70,000 reverses the direction; recording the change in OCI contradicts the general rule that valuation allowance changes from revised realizability judgments flow through continuing operations.
questions.explanationJa: 期末に必要な評価性引当金は実現が見込めない部分：600,000 × 25% = 150,000ドル。引当金は220,000→150,000ドルへ70,000ドル減少し、この減少は税金費用の貸方（減少）として計上される＝税金費用70,000ドルの減少。150,000ドルの増加は期末残高と増減額の混同、70,000ドルの増加は方向の誤り。実現可能性の判断変更による引当金の増減は原則として継続事業の税金費用で認識するため、OCI計上とする記述も誤りである。

questions.stem: Drummond Corp. takes a tax position with a total potential benefit of $1,000,000 and concludes the position is more likely than not to be sustained. Management estimates the following possible outcomes upon settlement: $1,000,000 sustained (30% probability), $700,000 sustained (30% probability), and $300,000 sustained (40% probability). What amount of tax benefit should Drummond recognize?
questions.explanation: Measurement uses the largest amount of benefit with a cumulative probability of realization greater than 50%. Cumulative probabilities: $1,000,000 → 30%; $700,000 → 30% + 30% = 60%. Since 60% exceeds 50%, $700,000 is recognized. Recognizing $1,000,000 ignores that the full amount has only a 30% cumulative likelihood; $630,000 is an expected (probability-weighted) value, which ASC 740 does not permit; $300,000 (100% cumulative) is overly conservative because the standard requires the largest amount exceeding 50%, not certainty.
questions.explanationJa: 測定は「実現の累積確率が50%を超える最大の便益額」で行う。累積確率は 1,000,000ドル → 30%、700,000ドル → 30% + 30% = 60%。60%は50%を超えるため、700,000ドルを認識する。1,000,000ドルの認識は累積確率が30%しかないことを無視した誤り、630,000ドルは期待値（確率加重平均）でありASC 740では認められない。300,000ドル（累積100%）は過度に保守的で、基準が要求するのは確実性ではなく「50%超となる最大額」である。

questions.subtopic: Intraperiod Allocation - Prior Period Error Correction
questions.stem: In Year 3, Ainsley Corp. discovers an error that understated Year 1 revenue by $200,000. The error is corrected as a prior period adjustment, and the additional income is also taxable, with an enacted rate of 25% applicable to the adjustment. How should the correction be reported in the Year 3 financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A $200,000 increase to beginning retained earnings, with $50,000 of tax expense in the Year 3 income statement

choices.label: B
choices.text: A $150,000 credit included in Year 3 income from continuing operations

choices.label: C
choices.text: A $150,000 increase to beginning retained earnings, net of $50,000 of tax

choices.label: D
choices.text: A $200,000 increase to beginning retained earnings with no tax effect

questions.explanation: Under intraperiod tax allocation, the tax effect of an item reported directly in retained earnings (a prior period adjustment) is also charged directly to retained earnings. The adjustment is presented net of tax: $200,000 × (1 − 25%) = $150,000 increase to beginning retained earnings. Splitting the tax into the current income statement mismatches the income and its tax; running the correction through continuing operations violates error-correction reporting; and omitting the tax effect ignores that the additional income is taxable.
questions.explanationJa: 期間内税金配分（intraperiod allocation）の下では、利益剰余金に直接計上される項目（過年度修正）の税効果も利益剰余金に直接賦課する。修正は税引後の純額で表示：200,000 × (1 − 25%) = 150,000ドルを期首利益剰余金の増加として計上する。税金だけを当期の損益計算書に計上するのは収益とその税効果の対応を崩す誤り、修正額を継続事業利益に含めるのは誤謬訂正の報告方法に反する。税効果を無視するのは追加収益が課税対象であることを見落としている。

questions.subtopic: DTL on Foreign Earnings - Withholding Tax Measurement
questions.stem: Renwick Corp.'s foreign subsidiary has $800,000 of undistributed earnings. Renwick does not assert indefinite reinvestment and expects the earnings to be remitted as dividends in the future. Under the applicable tax law, the dividends will be exempt from home-country income tax but will be subject to a 10% foreign dividend withholding tax for which no credit or refund is available. What deferred tax liability should Renwick recognize for the undistributed earnings?
questions.explanation: Because indefinite reinvestment is not asserted, a deferred tax liability is recognized for the tax expected to be incurred when the earnings are remitted. The only tax cost of remittance is the 10% withholding tax: $800,000 × 10% = $80,000. Recognizing nothing would require the indefinite reversal assertion, which Renwick has not made; $168,000 applies the 21% home-country rate to earnings that are exempt from home-country tax; $248,000 layers both taxes even though only the withholding tax will be incurred.
questions.explanationJa: 無期限再投資（indefinite reinvestment）を主張していないため、利益の送金時に発生が見込まれる税金について繰延税金負債を認識する。送金時の税コストは10%の源泉徴収税のみ：800,000 × 10% = 80,000ドル。負債ゼロとするには無期限再投資の主張が必要だが、本問ではしていない。168,000ドルは本国で非課税となる利益に本国税率21%を適用した誤り、248,000ドルは源泉税のみが発生するにもかかわらず両方の税を二重に積み上げた誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Reversal of DTL upon Asset Sale
questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 5, Ogden Corp. holds equipment with a book carrying amount of $240,000 and a tax basis of $180,000; the related deferred tax liability is $15,000 at the 25% enacted rate. During Year 5, Ogden sells the equipment to an unrelated party for $300,000. What is the effect of the sale on Year 5 deferred income tax expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A deferred tax benefit of $15,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: A deferred tax expense of $15,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: A deferred tax benefit of $60,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No effect on deferred tax expense

questions.explanation: The sale eliminates the temporary difference: the tax gain ($300,000 − $180,000 = $120,000) exceeds the book gain ($300,000 − $240,000 = $60,000) by the $60,000 cumulative difference, which is now taxed currently. The $15,000 deferred tax liability is therefore reversed, producing a deferred tax benefit of $15,000 (current tax expense rises correspondingly). Recording additional deferred expense doubles up the liability; $60,000 confuses the pretax difference with its tax effect; and asserting no effect overlooks that disposal triggers reversal of the entire difference.
questions.explanationJa: 売却により一時差異は消滅する。税務上の売却益（300,000 − 180,000 = 120,000ドル）は会計上の売却益（300,000 − 240,000 = 60,000ドル）を累積差異60,000ドル分上回り、その分が当期に課税される。よって繰延税金負債15,000ドルが取り崩され、繰延税金については15,000ドルのベネフィット（同額だけ当期税金費用は増加）となる。追加の繰延税金費用を計上するのは負債の二重計上、60,000ドルは税引前の差異額と税効果の混同、影響なしとするのは処分により差異全体が解消することの見落としである。

questions.stem: Lockwood Corp. enters Year 5 with a $700,000 post-2017 federal NOL carryforward. Year 5 taxable income before the NOL deduction is $600,000, and the NOL deduction is limited to 80% of taxable income. The enacted rate is 21% and no valuation allowance is required. What deferred tax asset related to the NOL carryforward should Lockwood report at the END of Year 5?
questions.explanation: The Year 5 NOL deduction is limited to 80% × $600,000 = $480,000. The remaining carryforward is $700,000 − $480,000 = $220,000, so the ending DTA = $220,000 × 21% = $46,200. The amount $147,000 is the beginning-of-year DTA ($700,000 × 21%) before any utilization; $0 assumes the entire NOL was absorbed, ignoring the 80% limitation; $25,200 is the Year 5 current tax payable ($120,000 of taxable income × 21%), not the deferred tax asset.
questions.explanationJa: Year 5のNOL控除は 80% × 600,000 = 480,000ドルに制限される。残存繰越額は 700,000 − 480,000 = 220,000ドルとなり、期末DTA = 220,000 × 21% = 46,200ドル。147,000ドルは利用前の期首DTA（700,000 × 21%）、0ドルは80%制限を無視してNOL全額が使用されたと仮定した誤り、25,200ドルはYear 5の当期未払税金（課税所得120,000 × 21%）であって繰延税金資産ではない。

questions.subtopic: Intraperiod Allocation - Loss from Continuing Operations Exception
questions.stem: In the current year, Pelham Corp. reports a pretax loss from continuing operations and pretax income from discontinued operations. Under the intraperiod tax allocation rules of ASC 740-20, how does the income from discontinued operations affect the tax benefit allocated to the continuing operations loss?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is ignored; each category is taxed on a strictly stand-alone basis

choices.label: B
choices.text: It must be considered as a source of income when determining the tax benefit allocated to the continuing operations loss

choices.label: C
choices.text: It eliminates any tax allocation to discontinued operations

choices.label: D
choices.text: It requires the entire tax provision to be reported in discontinued operations

questions.explanation: ASC 740-20-45-7 contains an exception to the incremental approach: when there is a loss from continuing operations, an entity must consider income in all other categories (such as discontinued operations or OCI) in determining the tax benefit allocated to continuing operations. This can result in recognizing a tax benefit on the continuing loss even when a valuation allowance might otherwise apply. Treating each category strictly stand-alone ignores this required exception, and the remaining descriptions misstate the mechanics — discontinued operations still receives its allocated tax, and the total provision is allocated among categories, not lumped into one.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-20-45-7 は増分アプローチの例外を定めている：継続事業が損失の場合、継続事業に配分する税ベネフィットの算定にあたり、他のすべての区分（非継続事業やOCIなど）の利益を考慮しなければならない。これにより、評価性引当金が必要な状況でも継続事業の損失に税ベネフィットが認識されることがある。各区分を完全に独立して課税計算するという記述はこの例外を無視しており、非継続事業への税配分が消えるという記述や税金全額を非継続事業に計上するという記述は配分の仕組みを誤っている。

questions.subtopic: Total Tax Expense with VA Change Component
questions.stem: For Year 2, Sutton Corp. computes current income tax payable of $150,000. During the year, deferred tax liabilities increased by $25,000, deferred tax assets increased by $40,000, and the valuation allowance increased by $10,000. What is Sutton's total income tax expense for Year 2?
questions.explanation: Deferred tax expense = increase in DTL ($25,000) − increase in DTA ($40,000) + increase in valuation allowance ($10,000) = −$5,000, a net deferred benefit. Total tax expense = $150,000 current − $5,000 = $145,000. Reporting $150,000 ignores all deferred components; $175,000 includes only the DTL increase ($150,000 + $25,000); $165,000 treats the valuation allowance increase as a reduction rather than an addition to expense, incorrectly computing $150,000 + $25,000 − $10,000 = $165,000 instead of the correct $150,000 + $25,000 + $10,000 − $40,000 = $145,000.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金費用 = DTL増加25,000 − DTA増加40,000 + 評価性引当金増加10,000 = △5,000ドル（純額でベネフィット）。総税金費用 = 当期分150,000 − 5,000 = 145,000ドル。150,000ドルは繰延部分をすべて無視、175,000ドルはDTL増加のみ反映（150,000 + 25,000）。165,000ドルは評価性引当金の増加を費用の増加要因ではなく減少要因として誤って計算した結果（150,000 + 25,000 − 10,000 = 165,000）で、DTA増加（費用の減少要因）も省略している。

questions.stem: Parent Corp. owns 100% of a domestic subsidiary whose financial reporting basis exceeds its tax basis due to undistributed earnings arising in fiscal years beginning after December 15, 1992. Under ASC 740-30, when may Parent omit a deferred tax liability for this outside basis difference?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the tax law provides a means (such as a tax-free liquidation or filing a consolidated return) by which the investment can be recovered tax-free and the entity expects to use that means

choices.label: B
choices.text: Never; a deferred tax liability is always required for domestic subsidiary earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only when the subsidiary has a history of paying no dividends

choices.label: D
choices.text: Whenever management believes the rate on dividends will decline in the future

questions.explanation: For a domestic subsidiary's post-1992 undistributed earnings, no deferred tax liability is recognized if the tax law provides a means of recovering the investment tax-free — for example, through a tax-free liquidation or because the entities file a consolidated return — and the entity expects to ultimately use that means. An absolute requirement to always record the liability ignores this exception. A mere history of not paying dividends is not the criterion (the test is a tax-free recovery mechanism, not dividend practice), and expectations about future rate declines are irrelevant to whether the liability is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 国内子会社の1992年以後発生の未分配利益については、税法上、投資を非課税で回収できる手段（例：非課税清算、連結納税申告）が存在し、かつ企業が最終的にその手段を用いる見込みである場合には、繰延税金負債を認識しなくてよい。常に負債計上が必要という記述はこの例外を無視している。配当を支払ってこなかった実績だけでは要件にならず（判断基準は非課税回収の手段の有無であって配当方針ではない）、将来の配当税率低下の見込みも負債認識の要否とは無関係である。

questions.subtopic: DTL Measured at Scheduled Future Enacted Rates
questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Hargrove Corp. has a $900,000 taxable temporary difference from an installment sale gain. Of this amount, $300,000 will reverse in Year 2 and $600,000 will reverse in Year 3 and later. Tax legislation already enacted at the balance sheet date sets the rate at 25% for Year 2 and 21% for Year 3 and all later years. What deferred tax liability should Hargrove report at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Deferred taxes are measured using the enacted rates expected to apply in the periods the differences reverse: ($300,000 × 25%) + ($600,000 × 21%) = $75,000 + $126,000 = $201,000. Using 25% for the entire difference ($225,000) ignores the enacted lower rate for later years; using 21% throughout ($189,000) applies the future rate to the Year 2 reversal; $213,000 swaps the rates between the two reversal layers.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金は、差異が解消する期間に適用される「制定済み」税率で測定する：(300,000 × 25%) + (600,000 × 21%) = 75,000 + 126,000 = 201,000ドル。全額に25%を使う225,000ドルは後年の制定済み低税率を無視、全額に21%を使う189,000ドルはYear 2解消分にまで将来税率を適用した誤り、213,000ドルは2つの解消年度の税率を取り違えた誤りである。

questions.subtopic: UTB Liability for Multiple Tax Positions
questions.stem: Calder Corp. has two uncertain tax positions at year-end. Position 1 claims a $300,000 benefit; it is more likely than not to be sustained, and the largest benefit with a cumulative probability greater than 50% is $200,000. Position 2 claims a $150,000 benefit; management concludes it has only a 40% likelihood of being sustained. What total liability for unrecognized tax benefits should Calder report?
questions.explanation: Position 1 meets the recognition threshold, so $200,000 is recognized and the unrecognized portion is $300,000 − $200,000 = $100,000. Position 2 fails the more-likely-than-not threshold, so no benefit is recognized and the full $150,000 is an unrecognized tax benefit. Total UTB liability = $100,000 + $150,000 = $250,000. Reporting $100,000 or $150,000 captures only one position, and $450,000 treats both claimed benefits as fully unrecognized even though $200,000 of Position 1 qualifies for recognition.
questions.explanationJa: ポジション1は認識基準（more-likely-than-not）を満たすため200,000ドルを認識し、未認識部分は 300,000 − 200,000 = 100,000ドル。ポジション2は50%超の基準を満たさないため便益は一切認識されず、150,000ドル全額が未認識税便益（UTB）となる。UTB負債合計 = 100,000 + 150,000 = 250,000ドル。100,000ドルや150,000ドルは片方のポジションしか反映していない。450,000ドルは、ポジション1のうち200,000ドルが認識可能であるにもかかわらず、両ポジションの申告便益全額を未認識とした誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Intraperiod Allocation with Continuing Loss and Discontinued Gain
questions.stem: For Year 1, Bramwell Corp. has a pretax loss from continuing operations of $200,000 and pretax income from discontinued operations of $500,000. The enacted tax rate on all items is 25%, there are no permanent or temporary differences, and total income tax expense for the year is $75,000 ($300,000 × 25%). Under intraperiod tax allocation, what tax amount is reported within continuing operations?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A tax benefit of $50,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Tax expense of $75,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Tax expense of $125,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0, because the company has overall taxable income

questions.explanation: Continuing operations is allocated the tax effect computed on its own pretax result first: a $200,000 loss × 25% = $50,000 tax benefit. The remainder of the total provision is allocated to discontinued operations: $75,000 − (−$50,000) = $125,000 expense, which nets to total tax of $75,000. Reporting the entire $75,000 in continuing operations fails to allocate; $125,000 is the discontinued operations allocation, not continuing; and allocating zero ignores that the continuing loss generates a benefit precisely because income in another category absorbs it.
questions.explanationJa: 期間内税金配分では、まず継続事業に対しその税引前損益に基づく税効果を配分する：損失200,000 × 25% = 50,000ドルの税ベネフィット。残額は非継続事業に配分：75,000 − (−50,000) = 125,000ドルの税金費用となり、合計で総税金費用75,000ドルと一致する。75,000ドル全額を継続事業に計上するのは配分を行っていない誤り、125,000ドルは非継続事業への配分額であって継続事業のものではない。ゼロとするのは、他区分の利益が損失を吸収するからこそ継続事業の損失にベネフィットが生じるという仕組みを無視している。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Expense Combining Rate Change and New Differences
questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 2, Eldridge Corp. has a deferred tax liability of $90,000, related to a $300,000 taxable temporary difference measured at the 30% enacted rate. During Year 2, a new rate of 25% is enacted effective immediately, none of the existing difference reverses, and a new taxable temporary difference of $200,000 originates. What is Eldridge's total deferred income tax expense for Year 2?
questions.explanation: The ending DTL = total taxable differences of $500,000 × 25% = $125,000. Deferred tax expense = ending $125,000 − beginning $90,000 = $35,000. This combines a $15,000 remeasurement benefit on the old difference ($300,000 × 5% rate decrease) with $50,000 of expense on the new difference ($200,000 × 25%). The amount $50,000 ignores the remeasurement benefit; $15,000 is only the rate-change effect (and as a benefit, not expense); $60,000 measures the new difference at the superseded 30% rate.
questions.explanationJa: 期末DTL = 将来加算一時差異合計500,000 × 25% = 125,000ドル。繰延税金費用 = 期末125,000 − 期首90,000 = 35,000ドル。内訳は、既存差異の再測定によるベネフィット15,000ドル（300,000 × 税率低下5%）と、新規差異に係る費用50,000ドル（200,000 × 25%）の合成である。50,000ドルは再測定ベネフィットの無視、15,000ドルは税率変更の影響のみ（しかも費用ではなくベネフィット）、60,000ドルは新規差異を旧税率30%で測定した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Valuation Allowance with 80% Limitation and Reversing DTL Source
questions.stem: Fenwick Corp. has a $700,000 post-2017 NOL carryforward (indefinite life), producing a gross deferred tax asset of $147,000 at the 21% enacted rate. The only source of future taxable income is the scheduled reversal of existing taxable temporary differences totaling $500,000. The NOL deduction in any future year is limited to 80% of taxable income. What valuation allowance should Fenwick report?
questions.explanation: The reversing taxable differences generate $500,000 of future taxable income, but the NOL can offset only 80% of it: $500,000 × 80% = $400,000 of usable NOL. Realizable DTA = $400,000 × 21% = $84,000, so the valuation allowance = $147,000 − $84,000 = $63,000. No allowance overstates realization; a full allowance ignores the reversing DTLs as a source of income; $42,000 ($147,000 − $500,000 × 21%) forgets the 80% limitation on how much income the NOL can shelter.
questions.explanationJa: 将来加算一時差異の解消により将来課税所得500,000ドルが生じるが、NOLで相殺できるのはその80%まで：500,000 × 80% = 400,000ドル。実現可能なDTA = 400,000 × 21% = 84,000ドル、よって評価性引当金 = 147,000 − 84,000 = 63,000ドル。引当金ゼロは実現可能性の過大評価、全額147,000ドルは所得源泉としてのDTL解消を無視した誤り。42,000ドル（147,000 − 500,000 × 21%）は、NOLが相殺できる所得に80%の制限があることを忘れた誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Intra-Entity Transfer of Non-Inventory Asset - Buyer DTA
questions.stem: Parent Corp. sells equipment (a non-inventory asset) to its wholly owned consolidated subsidiary at an intra-entity gain of $200,000. Parent pays current tax of $50,000 on the gain in its jurisdiction. The subsidiary's tax basis in the equipment is stepped up by the $200,000 gain, and the enacted rate in the subsidiary's jurisdiction is 30%. Under ASU 2016-16, what deferred tax asset should the consolidated group recognize at the date of transfer?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $0; recognition of all tax effects is deferred until the asset is sold to an outside party

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-16 requires the income tax consequences of intra-entity transfers of assets other than inventory to be recognized when the transfer occurs. The buyer's tax basis exceeds the consolidated book carrying amount (the intra-entity gain is eliminated in consolidation) by $200,000, so a deferred tax asset of $200,000 × 30% = $60,000 is recognized; the seller's $50,000 current tax is also expensed currently. Deferring all tax effects describes the treatment that applies only to inventory transfers; $50,000 is the seller's current tax, not the buyer's DTA; $10,000 improperly nets the two amounts into a single figure.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-16 により、棚卸資産以外の資産のグループ内移転に係る税効果は移転時に認識する。買手の税務基準額は連結上の帳簿価額（グループ内利益は連結で消去）を200,000ドル上回るため、繰延税金資産 = 200,000 × 30% = 60,000ドルを認識し、売手の当期税金50,000ドルも当期に費用処理する。すべての税効果を外部売却まで繰り延べる処理は棚卸資産の移転のみに残された例外である。50,000ドルは売手の当期税金であって買手のDTAではなく、10,000ドルは両者を不適切に相殺した数値である。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Liability – Enacted Rate at Reversal
questions.stem:
Frey Corp., a construction company, appropriately uses the completed contract method of accounting for income tax purposes. However, Frey uses the percentage of completion accounting method for financial statement purposes. Pertinent data at December 31, 2002, the close of Frey's first year of operations, are:

| Date contract began | Estimated completion date | Income recognized in 2002 |
|---|---|---|
| 3/1/02 | 9/1/03 | $600,000 |
| 6/1/02 | 12/1/03 | $300,000 |
| 9/1/02 | 3/1/04 | $200,000 |
| 12/1/02 | 6/1/04 | $100,000 |

Frey's enacted income tax rates are 30% for 2002, 25% for 2003 and 20% for 2004. Frey elected early application of FASB Statement No. 109, Accounting for Income Taxes, in its financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2002. Assuming Frey expects taxable income in all future periods, what amount should be included in the deferred income tax liability at December 31, 2002, for these transactions?
questions.explanation: Under the percentage-of-completion method, Frey recognizes income currently on its books but defers that same income for tax purposes under the completed-contract method. The deferred tax liability (DTL) must be measured using the enacted tax rates in effect when each temporary difference is expected to reverse. Contracts completing in 2003 ($600,000 + $300,000 = $900,000) will reverse at the enacted 2003 rate of 25%, generating $225,000 of DTL. Contracts completing in 2004 ($200,000 + $100,000 = $300,000) will reverse at the enacted 2004 rate of 20%, generating $60,000 of DTL. Total DTL = $225,000 + $60,000 = $285,000.
questions.explanationJa: 工事進行基準では、Freyは当期に財務諸表上で収益を認識するが、税務上は工事完成基準のため収益認識が繰り延べられる。繰延税金負債（DTL）は、各一時差異が解消される年の制定税率で測定する。2003年完成の工事（$600,000＋$300,000＝$900,000）は25%の税率で解消され、$225,000のDTL。2004年完成の工事（$200,000＋$100,000＝$300,000）は20%の税率で解消され、$60,000のDTL。合計DTL＝$225,000＋$60,000＝$285,000。

questions.subtopic: Current Income Tax Liability – Taxable Income Calculation
questions.stem: On January 2, Year 1, Ross Co. purchased a machine for $70,000. This machine has a 5-year useful life and a salvage value of $10,000, and it is depreciated using the straight-line method for financial statement purposes. For tax purposes, depreciation expense was $25,000 for Year 1 and $20,000 for Year 2. Ross' Year 2 income, before income taxes and depreciation expense, was $100,000 and its effective tax rate was 30%. If Ross had made no estimated tax payments during Year 2, what amount of current income tax liability would Ross report in its December 31, Year 2, balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Straight-line book depreciation = ($70,000 – $10,000) ÷ 5 = $12,000 per year. Pretax book income for Year 2 = $100,000 – $12,000 = $88,000. For tax purposes, taxable income = $100,000 – $20,000 (tax depreciation Year 2) = $80,000. Current income tax liability = $80,000 × 30% = $24,000. Since no estimated payments were made, the full $24,000 is the current income tax liability on the balance sheet.
questions.explanationJa: 定額法による財務諸表上の減価償却費＝（$70,000－$10,000）÷5年＝$12,000。Year 2の税引前利益＝$100,000－$12,000＝$88,000。課税所得＝$100,000－$20,000（税務上の減価償却費）＝$80,000。当期法人税負債＝$80,000×30%＝$24,000。見積前払税金がないため、貸借対照表上の当期法人税負債は$24,000となる。

questions.subtopic: Current Income Tax Expense – Multiple Temporary Differences
questions.stem: Leer Corp.'s pretax income in Year 1 was $100,000. The temporary differences between amounts reported in the financial statements and the tax return are as follows: Depreciation in the financial statements was $8,000 more than tax depreciation. The equity method of accounting resulted in financial statement income of $35,000 from the investee. A $25,000 dividend was received during the year from the investee, which is eligible for the 65% dividends received deduction. Leer's effective income tax rate was 30% in Year 1. In its Year 1 income statement, Leer should report a current income tax expense of
questions.explanation: Current income tax expense is based on taxable income. Starting with pretax book income of $100,000: (1) Book depreciation is $8,000 more than tax depreciation, so taxable income is $8,000 higher → add $8,000; (2) Equity method income of $35,000 is recognized in the books but not currently taxable → subtract $35,000; (3) Dividends received of $25,000 are taxable, reduced by the 65% dividends-received deduction, leaving 35% taxable: $25,000 × 35% = $8,750 → add $8,750. Taxable income = $100,000 + $8,000 – $35,000 + $8,750 = $81,750. Current income tax expense = $81,750 × 30% = $24,525.
questions.explanationJa: 当期法人税費用は課税所得に基づく。税引前利益$100,000から出発して：(1) 財務諸表の減価償却費が税務より$8,000多い→課税所得は$8,000多くなる（加算）；(2) 持分法による利益$35,000は帳簿上認識されるが現時点では非課税→減算$35,000；(3) 受取配当$25,000は課税対象で65%の配当控除後、課税対象部分＝$25,000×35%＝$8,750（加算）。課税所得＝$100,000＋$8,000－$35,000＋$8,750＝$81,750。当期法人税費用＝$81,750×30%＝$24,525。

questions.subtopic: Net Deferred Tax Asset/Liability – Balance Sheet Classification
questions.stem:
Rom Corp. began business in Year 1 and reported taxable income of $50,000 on its Year 1 tax return. Rom's enacted tax rate is 30% for Year 1 and future years. The following is a schedule of Rom's December 31, Year 1, temporary differences in thousands of dollars: 12/31/Y1 book basis Future taxable (deductible) amounts over (under) tax basis

|  | Year 2 | Year 3 |
|------|------|------|
| Year 4 Year 5 Equipment | 10 | (5) |

5 5 5 Warranty liability (20) (10) (10) Deferred compensation liability (15) (5) (10) Revenue from installment sales 30 10 20 Totals 5 (5) (10) 25 (5) What amount should Rom report as net deferred tax liability in its December 31, Year 1, balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740, deferred tax assets and liabilities are measured on all temporary differences. Future taxable amounts create DTLs; future deductible amounts create DTAs. Summing across all future years: future taxable amounts = $25,000 (Year 4); future deductible amounts = $5,000 (Year 2) + $10,000 (Year 3) + $5,000 (Year 5) = $20,000 (in thousands). DTL = $25,000 × 30% = $7,500; DTA = $20,000 × 30% = $6,000. Net DTL = $7,500 – $6,000 = $1,500 (in thousands = $1,500).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740の下では、すべての一時差異について繰延税金資産・負債を認識する。将来課税（taxable）の差異はDTL、将来控除（deductible）の差異はDTAとなる。将来課税合計：$25,000（Year 4）。将来控除合計：$5,000（Year 2）＋$10,000（Year 3）＋$5,000（Year 5）＝$20,000（千ドル単位）。DTL＝$25,000×30%＝$7,500、DTA＝$20,000×30%＝$6,000。純繰延税金負債＝$7,500－$6,000＝$1,500（千ドル）。

questions.subtopic: Current Tax Expense – Permanent and Temporary Differences
questions.stem:
For the year ended December 31, Year 4, Grim Co.'s pretax accounting income was $200,000, which included the following:

| Item | Amount |
|------|--------|
| Meals | $30,000 |
| Fines | 6,000 |
| Warranty expense | 40,000 |
| For tax purposes assume only | 50 |

% of the deduction for meals is permitted. The warranty expense includes $18,000 of warranty costs paid during the year. Grim's enacted income tax rate is 30%. In its Year 4 income statement, what amount should Grim report as the current portion of its provision for income tax expense?
questions.explanation: The current portion of income tax expense is based on taxable income for Year 4. Starting from pretax book income of $200,000, three adjustments are required: (1) Meals: book deduction is $30,000, but only 50% ($15,000) is tax-deductible, so the non-deductible $15,000 is a permanent difference that increases taxable income; (2) Fines of $6,000 are never tax-deductible (permanent difference), adding $6,000 to taxable income; (3) Warranty expense: $40,000 was expensed on the books, but only the $18,000 actually paid is deductible for tax, so $22,000 is added back to taxable income. Taxable income = $200,000 + $15,000 + $6,000 + $22,000 = $243,000. Current income tax = $243,000 × 30% = $72,900.
questions.explanationJa: 当期法人税費用はYear 4の課税所得に基づく。税引前利益$200,000から：(1) 食事代：帳簿上$30,000の費用のうち、税務上50%（$15,000）のみ控除可能。$15,000は永久差異として課税所得を増加させる；(2) 罰金$6,000は永久的に税務上非控除、$6,000加算；(3) 品質保証費：帳簿上$40,000を費用計上、税務上は支払済の$18,000のみ控除可能、$22,000加算。課税所得＝$200,000＋$15,000＋$6,000＋$22,000＝$243,000。当期法人税費用＝$243,000×30%＝$72,900。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Liability – Future Enacted Rates
questions.stem: In its Year 1 income statement, Tow, Inc. reported proceeds from an officer's life insurance policy of $90,000 and depreciation of $250,000. Tow was the owner and beneficiary of the life insurance on its officer. Tow deducted depreciation of $370,000 in its Year 1 income tax return when the tax rate was 30%. Data related to the reversal of the excess tax deduction for depreciation follow: Year Reversal of excess tax deduction Enacted tax rates Year 2 $50,000 35% Year 3 $40,000 35% Year 4 $20,000 25% Year 5 $10,000 25% There are no other temporary differences. In its December 31, Year 1, balance sheet, what amount should Tow report as a deferred income tax liability?
questions.explanation: The life insurance proceeds of $90,000 are permanently excluded from taxable income and create no deferred tax. The excess tax depreciation over book depreciation ($370,000 – $250,000 = $120,000) creates a deferred tax liability because the tax deduction is taken now but will reverse as future taxable income. Under ASC 740, the DTL is measured at the enacted tax rates that will apply when each reversal occurs: Year 2 reversal $50,000 × 35% = $17,500; Year 3 reversal $40,000 × 35% = $14,000; Year 4 reversal $20,000 × 25% = $5,000; Year 5 reversal $10,000 × 25% = $2,500. Total DTL = $17,500 + $14,000 + $5,000 + $2,500 = $39,000.
questions.explanationJa: 生命保険金$90,000は課税所得から永久に除外され、繰延税金は発生しない。税務上の減価償却費が帳簿上を上回る額（$370,000－$250,000＝$120,000）は、現在税務控除を取るが将来課税所得として解消されるため、DTLとなる。ASC 740の下、DTLは各解消年の制定税率で測定する：Year 2 $50,000×35%＝$17,500、Year 3 $40,000×35%＝$14,000、Year 4 $20,000×25%＝$5,000、Year 5 $10,000×25%＝$2,500。合計DTL＝$39,000。

questions.subtopic: Balance Sheet Classification – Noncurrent Deferred Tax
questions.stem: Lark Co.'s trial balance included the following at December 31, Year 5: Accounts payable $75,000 Bonds payable due Year 7 450,000 Discount on bonds payable 22,000 Deferred income tax liability 30,000 What amount should be included in the noncurrent liability section of Lark's December 31, Year 5, balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Accounts payable of $75,000 is a current liability and is excluded.
