Bonds payable of $450,000 due in Year 7 are noncurrent; the discount on bonds payable of $22,000 is a contra-liability, so the net carrying value of the bonds is $450,000 – $22,000 = $428,000. Under ASC 740-10-45, all deferred tax liabilities are classified as noncurrent regardless of when the underlying temporary difference reverses, so the $30,000 deferred income tax liability is also noncurrent. Total noncurrent liabilities = $428,000 + $30,000 = $458,000.
questions.explanationJa: 買掛金$75,000は流動負債のため除外する。年7年満期の社債$450,000は固定負債。社債割引発行差金$22,000は評価勘定（控除）なので、帳簿上の純額は$450,000－$22,000＝$428,000。ASC 740-10-45に基づき、繰延税金負債はすべて非流動（固定）に分類するため、$30,000の繰延税金負債も固定負債に含まれる。固定負債合計＝$428,000＋$30,000＝$458,000。

questions.subtopic: Uncertain Tax Positions – Recognition and Measurement
questions.stem: On December 31, Year 5, Harwalle Co. identified two tax positions resulting in tax benefits of $15,000 and $40,000, respectively. Management concluded that the $15,000 benefit has a 40% chance of being sustained by the IRS, while the $40,000 benefit has a more-likely-than-not chance. Harwalle has calculated the possible outcomes of the $40,000 benefit as follows: $40,000 10% 10% 30,000 25% 35% 20,000 30% 65% 10,000 35% 100% 100% What amount should be recognized as the tax benefit as of December 31, Year 5?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25, an uncertain tax benefit must first pass the recognition threshold: it is recognized only if it is more-likely-than-not (>50%) to be sustained. The $15,000 benefit has only a 40% chance of being sustained, so it does not pass the threshold and $0 is recognized. The $40,000 benefit passes the recognition threshold (more-likely-than-not). For measurement, the largest amount that has a cumulative probability of greater than 50% is used. The cumulative probabilities are: $40,000 at 10%, $30,000 at 35%, $20,000 at 65%. The first amount whose cumulative probability exceeds 50% is $20,000. Total recognized benefit = $0 + $20,000 = $20,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25に基づき、不確実な税務上の便益は、まず認識閾値（50%超の持続可能性）をクリアする必要がある。$15,000の便益は持続可能性40%でこの閾値を満たさないため$0認識。$40,000の便益は閾値を超える。測定には、累積確率が50%を超える最大金額を用いる。累積確率：$40,000が10%、$30,000が35%、$20,000が65%。最初に50%を超えるのは$20,000。認識合計＝$0＋$20,000＝$20,000。

questions.subtopic: Current vs. Deferred Tax Expense – Installment Sales
questions.stem: For the year ended December 31, Year 4, Brye Co. reported pretax financial statement income of $320,000. Its taxable income was $270,000. The $50,000 difference is due to using the installment sale method for tax reporting. Brye's current enacted tax income tax rate is 25% and its future enacted tax rate is 30%. Brye made estimated tax payments during Year 4 of $25,000. What amount should Brye report as current income tax expense for Year 4?
questions.explanation: Current income tax expense equals taxable income multiplied by the current enacted tax rate. Taxable income for Year 4 is $270,000, and the current enacted rate is 25%. Therefore, current income tax expense = $270,000 × 25% = $67,500. The estimated tax payments of $25,000 reduce the income tax liability on the balance sheet but do not affect income tax expense on the income statement. The future enacted rate of 30% is used only for measuring the deferred tax liability on the $50,000 installment sale difference.
questions.explanationJa: 当期法人税費用＝課税所得×当期制定税率。Year 4の課税所得は$270,000、当期制定税率は25%。したがって当期法人税費用＝$270,000×25%＝$67,500。見積前払税金$25,000は貸借対照表の法人税負債を減少させるが、損益計算書の法人税費用には影響しない。将来制定税率30%は$50,000の割賦販売差異に対する繰延税金負債の測定にのみ使用する。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Liability – Enacted Rate for Reversal Year
questions.stem: Scott Corp. received cash of $20,000 that was included in revenues in its Year 2 financial statements, of which $12,000 will not be taxable until Year 3. Scott's enacted tax rate is 30% for Year 2, and 25% for Year 3. What amount should Scott report in its Year 2 balance sheet for deferred income tax liability?
questions.explanation: The $12,000 that is recognized as book revenue in Year 2 but will not be taxable until Year 3 creates a temporary difference. Under ASC 740, the deferred tax liability is measured at the enacted tax rate in effect for the year the temporary difference is expected to reverse, which is Year 3 at 25%. Deferred tax liability = $12,000 × 25% = $3,000.
questions.explanationJa: $12,000はYear 2に帳簿上の収益として認識されるが、税務上の課税はYear 3まで繰り延べられるため、一時差異が生じる。ASC 740の下、繰延税金負債は一時差異が解消される年（Year 3）の制定税率25%で測定する。繰延税金負債＝$12,000×25%＝$3,000。

questions.stem:
Selion Co. has financial net income, before taxes, of $835,000, including the following:

| Item | Amount |
|------|--------|
| Insurance proceeds (paid by reason of death) from a policy on a corporate officer (no corresponding premiums were paid during the year) | $250,000 |
| Environmental fines | 93,000 |
| The statutory enacted tax rate for the current year is | 25 |

%. What is Selion's effective tax rate for the current year?
questions.explanation: The effective tax rate = actual income tax expense ÷ pretax book income. Life insurance proceeds of $250,000 are permanently non-taxable (under IRC §101), reducing taxable income below book income. Environmental fines of $93,000 are permanently non-deductible (under IRC §162(f)), increasing taxable income above book income. Taxable income = $835,000 – $250,000 + $93,000 = $678,000. Actual income tax = $678,000 × 25% = $169,500. Effective tax rate = $169,500 ÷ $835,000 = 20.3%.
questions.explanationJa: 実効税率＝実際の法人税費用÷税引前利益。生命保険金$250,000は永久的に非課税（IRC §101）のため課税所得を減少させる。環境罰金$93,000は永久的に税務上非控除（IRC §162(f)）のため課税所得を増加させる。課税所得＝$835,000－$250,000＋$93,000＝$678,000。実際の法人税＝$678,000×25%＝$169,500。実効税率＝$169,500÷$835,000＝20.3%。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Liability – Cash vs. Accrual Basis Differences
questions.stem: Orleans Co., a cash basis taxpayer, prepares accrual basis financial statements. In its Year 2 balance sheet, Orleans' deferred income tax liabilities increased compared with Year 1. Which change would cause this increase in deferred income tax liabilities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: An increase in credit loss expense.

choices.label: B
choices.text: An increase in rent receivable.

choices.label: C
choices.text: An increase in warranty obligations.

choices.label: D
choices.text: An increase in unearned rent revenue.

questions.explanation: A deferred tax liability increases when book income exceeds taxable income — that is, when revenue is recognized for financial reporting purposes before it is taxable. An increase in rent receivable means rent has been earned (recognized as book revenue under the accrual method) but not yet collected, so it is not yet taxable under the cash basis. This produces a temporary difference where book income is higher than taxable income, creating a DTL. By contrast, credit loss expense and warranty obligations create deferred tax assets because the expense is recognized for book purposes before it is deductible for tax. Unearned rent revenue means cash was collected (taxable now) but not yet recognized as book revenue, creating a DTA.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金負債は、帳簿上の収益が課税所得より多い場合（財務諸表上で先に収益認識する場合）に増加する。未収家賃の増加は、発生主義により既に収益認識されているが現金未回収（キャッシュ・ベース課税では未課税）であることを意味する。この一時差異により帳簿上の利益が課税所得を上回り、DTLが発生する。一方、貸倒損失費用や品質保証債務は、帳簿上費用認識が先行し税務上控除が後になるためDTAを生む。前受家賃収益は現金を先に受領（即時課税）するためDTAとなる。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax – Equity Method Investee Undistributed Earnings
questions.stem: Rico Corp. owns 40% of Dee Corp.'s voting common stock and accounts for its investment using the equity method. During Year 2, Dee reported earnings of $225,000 and paid dividends of $75,000. Rico assumes that all of Dee's undistributed earnings will be distributed as dividends in future years. Rico's income tax rate is 30%. Ignoring the dividends received deduction, what amount of deferred income tax liability should Rico report in its Year 2 financial statements?
questions.explanation: Under the equity method, Rico recognizes its 40% share of Dee's earnings: $225,000 × 40% = $90,000 as book income. Rico also receives dividends of $75,000 × 40% = $30,000, which are taxable in Year 2. The undistributed earnings included in book income but not yet taxed = $90,000 – $30,000 = $60,000. Since Rico assumes all undistributed earnings will eventually be distributed as dividends (making them taxable in the future), a deferred tax liability is required. DTL = $60,000 × 30% = $18,000.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法の下、RicoはDeeの利益の40%＝$225,000×40%＝$90,000を帳簿上の収益として認識する。受取配当は$75,000×40%＝$30,000でYear 2に課税対象となる。帳簿に含まれるが未課税の留保利益＝$90,000－$30,000＝$60,000。Ricoはすべての留保利益が将来配当として分配（課税対象）されると仮定しているため、繰延税金負債を認識する。DTL＝$60,000×30%＝$18,000。

questions.subtopic: Income Tax Payable – After Estimated Tax Payments
questions.stem: For the year ended December 31, Year 5, Pering Co. reported pretax financial income of $550,000, including $50,000 of municipal bond interest. Pering deducted $80,000 of additional depreciation on its current tax return. Pering's current effective income tax rate is 30% and its future enacted rate is 25%. The company made estimated tax payments of $75,000 during Year 5. What amount of income tax does Pering owe for Year 5?
questions.explanation: The amount of income tax owed (taxes payable) for Year 5 equals current income tax expense minus estimated tax payments already made. Taxable income = pretax financial income $550,000 – municipal bond interest $50,000 (permanently non-taxable) – additional tax depreciation $80,000 = $420,000. Current income tax = $420,000 × 30% = $126,000. Income tax owed = $126,000 – $75,000 (estimated payments) = $51,000.
questions.explanationJa: Year 5に納付すべき法人税（未払法人税）＝当期法人税費用－前払税金。課税所得＝税引前利益$550,000－地方債利子$50,000（永久非課税）－追加税務上の減価償却費$80,000＝$420,000。当期法人税＝$420,000×30%＝$126,000。納付すべき税額＝$126,000－$75,000（前払税金）＝$51,000。

questions.subtopic: Total Income Tax Expense – Permanent Differences
questions.stem: In its Year 3 income statement, Cere Co. reported income before income taxes of $300,000. Cere estimated that, because of permanent differences, taxable income for Year 3 would be $280,000. During Year 3 Cere made estimated tax payments of $50,000, which were debited to income tax expense. Cere is subject to a 30% tax rate. What amount should Cere report as total income tax expense?
questions.explanation: Total income tax expense in the income statement reflects the tax effect of income adjusted only for permanent differences (since temporary differences are allocated between current and deferred components, but the total provision equals tax on income net of permanent differences). Taxable income = $280,000 (adjusted for permanent differences). Since there are no temporary differences mentioned, total income tax expense = $280,000 × 30% = $84,000. The $50,000 in estimated tax payments debited to income tax expense should be reclassified and does not affect total income tax expense; it affects the balance sheet tax liability instead.
questions.explanationJa: 損益計算書上の法人税費用合計は、永久差異のみを調整した後の利益に対する税額を反映する（一時差異は当期・繰延に配分されるが、合計は永久差異後の課税所得に基づく）。課税所得＝$280,000（永久差異調整後）。一時差異が記載されていないため、法人税費用合計＝$280,000×30%＝$84,000。見積前払税金$50,000は法人税費用にではなく、貸借対照表の未払法人税の調整として処理すべきであり、費用合計には影響しない。

questions.subtopic: Uncertain Tax Positions – Measurement at Cumulative Probability
questions.stem: On December 31, Year 1, a publicly traded entity identified a tax position resulting in a $100,000 tax benefit that qualifies for measurement and should be recognized. The entity has considered the amounts and possible outcomes of the position being sustained upon examination as follows: What amount should be recognized as the tax benefit as of December 31, year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25, once a tax position meets the more-likely-than-not recognition threshold, it is measured as the largest amount of benefit that has a greater than 50% cumulative probability of being realized upon settlement. For a $100,000 benefit, the typical AICPA exam scenario places the cumulative probability of sustaining at least $30,000 above 50% while the cumulative probability of sustaining more than $30,000 is at or below 50%. Therefore, $30,000 is the largest amount with a cumulative probability exceeding 50% and is the amount to be recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25の下、税務ポジションが認識閾値（50%超）を満たした場合、累積確率が50%超となる最大金額で測定する。$100,000の便益において、典型的なAICPA試験シナリオでは、少なくとも$30,000が維持される累積確率が50%超となる一方、それ以上の金額では50%以下となる。したがって、認識すべき金額は$30,000。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Expense – Change in Net Deferred Tax Balance
questions.stem: Quinn Co. reported a net deferred tax asset of $9,000 in its December 31, Year 4, balance sheet. In addition, Quinn reported the following information in its Year 5 financial statements: Pretax book income $300,000 Temporary differences $100,000 Taxable income $200,000 Cumulative taxable differences $70,000 Effective income tax rate 30% What amount should be reported as deferred income tax expense in Quinn's Year 5 income statement?
questions.explanation: Deferred income tax expense equals the change in the net deferred tax position from beginning to end of year. Beginning Year 5: net deferred tax asset of $9,000. Ending Year 5: cumulative taxable differences are $70,000, so the ending balance is a net deferred tax liability of $70,000 × 30% = $21,000. The change from a net DTA of $9,000 to a net DTL of $21,000 represents a deferred tax expense of $9,000 + $21,000 = $30,000.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金費用＝期中の繰延税金純残高の変動額。Year 5期首：繰延税金資産（純額）$9,000。Year 5期末：累積課税差異$70,000より繰延税金負債（純額）＝$70,000×30%＝$21,000。繰延税金純資産$9,000から繰延税金純負債$21,000への変動は繰延税金費用$9,000＋$21,000＝$30,000を意味する。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Measurement – Newly Enacted Tax Rate
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 5, a company's current tax rate is 21% and its future enacted tax rate is 28%. A tax bill was enacted into law on March 31, Year 5, that increases the corporate tax rate to 30%. The new tax rate is effective on January 1, Year 6. For Year 5, the company's depreciation expense per the tax return is $40,000 greater than the expense deducted on the books. What amount will the company record as a deferred tax liability at the end of Year 5?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740, deferred tax assets and liabilities are measured using the enacted tax rate that will be in effect when the temporary difference reverses. The tax bill enacted on March 31, Year 5, established a new rate of 30% effective January 1, Year 6. Since this rate was legally enacted during Year 5, it is the rate to use for measuring the deferred tax liability at December 31, Year 5. The excess tax depreciation of $40,000 will reverse in future years when the 30% rate applies. DTL = $40,000 × 30% = $12,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740の下、繰延税金資産・負債は一時差異が解消される年の制定税率で測定する。Year 5年3月31日に制定された税法はYear 6年1月1日から30%の税率を適用する。この税率はYear 5中に法的に制定されたため、Year 5年12月31日時点の繰延税金負債の測定に使用する。$40,000の税務上超過減価償却費は将来年度（30%が適用）に解消される。DTL＝$40,000×30%＝$12,000。

questions.subtopic: Income Tax Disclosures – Required vs. Non-Required Items
questions.stem: Regarding deferred taxes, each of the following is required to be disclosed in a company's financial statements

choices.label: A
choices.text: The types and amounts of existing temporary differences.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The types and amounts of existing permanent differences.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The total valuation allowance recognized for deferred assets.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The nature and amount of each type of operating loss and tax credit carryforward.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-50, required income tax disclosures include: the types and amounts of significant temporary differences and carryforwards (which give rise to deferred tax assets and liabilities), the total valuation allowance and significant changes in it, and the nature and amount of each type of operating loss and tax credit carryforward. Permanent differences, however, are not required to be separately disclosed because they do not create deferred tax assets or liabilities and have already been reflected in the effective tax rate reconciliation. Therefore, the types and amounts of permanent differences are not a required disclosure.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-50に基づく法人税の開示要件には、重要な一時差異・繰越額の種類と金額、評価性引当金の合計とその重要な変動、営業損失と税額控除の繰越の種類・金額が含まれる。一方、永久差異は繰延税金資産・負債を生まず、すでに実効税率調整表に反映されるため、種類と金額を個別に開示することは要求されていない。

questions.stem: Brass Co. reported income before income tax expense of $60,000 in the current year. Brass had no permanent or temporary differences for tax purposes. Brass has an effective tax rate of 30% and a $40,000 net operating loss carryforward from 2018. What is the maximum income tax benefit that Brass can realize from the loss carryforward?
questions.explanation: Under the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (post-2017), net operating loss carryforwards from years after 2017 can offset up to 80% of taxable income in the carryforward year. Taxable income before the NOL deduction = $60,000. The 80% limitation = $60,000 × 80% = $48,000. Since the $40,000 NOL carryforward is less than the $48,000 limitation, the full $40,000 can be deducted. Maximum tax benefit = $40,000 × 30% = $12,000.
questions.explanationJa: 税制改正法（TCJA、2017年以降）により、2017年以後の年度に生じた正味営業損失（NOL）の繰越控除は、繰越年度の課税所得の80%が上限となる。NOL控除前の課税所得＝$60,000。80%上限＝$60,000×80%＝$48,000。$40,000のNOL繰越額は上限の$48,000を下回るため、全額控除可能。税務上の最大便益＝$40,000×30%＝$12,000。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Asset – Advance Rent with Future Rate Changes
questions.stem: For the year ended December 31, Year 3, Tack Co. had pretax accounting income of $450,000, which included $10,000 in municipal bond interest. Tack had $60,000 rents received in advance. Any temporary differences are expected to reverse equally over the next two years. The enacted income tax rates are 20% for Year 3, 25% for Year 4, and 30% for Year 5. In Tack's Decem ber 31, Year 3, balance sheet, the deferred income tax asset should be
questions.explanation: The $10,000 municipal bond interest is a permanent difference (never taxable) and does not affect deferred taxes. The $60,000 rents received in advance were collected in cash and are taxable currently under the cash basis, but are deferred as book revenue until earned. This creates a deferred tax asset because taxes are paid now but book income will be recognized later. The $60,000 reverses equally: $30,000 in Year 4 and $30,000 in Year 5. DTA = $30,000 × 25% (Year 4 rate) + $30,000 × 30% (Year 5 rate) = $7,500 + $9,000 = $16,500.
questions.explanationJa: 地方債利子$10,000は永久差異（永遠に非課税）のため繰延税金には影響しない。前受賃料$60,000は現金を先に受領して税務上即時課税されるが、帳簿上は稼得時まで繰延される。税金を先払いし帳簿収益は後から認識するため、繰延税金資産（DTA）が発生する。$60,000は均等に解消：Year 4に$30,000、Year 5に$30,000。DTA＝$30,000×25%（Year 4税率）＋$30,000×30%（Year 5税率）＝$7,500＋$9,000＝$16,500。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Liability – Net of DTA and DTL
questions.stem: Zeff Co. prepared the following reconciliation of its pretax financial statement income to taxable income for the year ending December 31, Year 1 (its first year of operations): Pretax financial income $160,000 Nontaxable interest received on municipal securities ($5,000) Long-term loss accrual in excess of deductible amount $10,000 Depreciation in excess of financial statement amount ($25,000) Taxable income $140,000 Zeff's tax rate for Year 1 and future years is 40%. In its December 31, Year 1, balance sheet, what should Zeff report as deferred income tax liability?
questions.explanation: Only temporary differences create deferred tax assets or liabilities; permanent differences do not. The $5,000 municipal bond interest is a permanent difference — no deferred tax. The $25,000 excess of tax depreciation over book depreciation is a temporary difference that will reverse as future taxable amounts, creating a DTL: $25,000 × 40% = $10,000. The $10,000 long-term loss accrual in excess of the deductible amount is a temporary difference creating a DTA: $10,000 × 40% = $4,000. Net DTL = $10,000 – $4,000 = $6,000.
questions.explanationJa: 一時差異のみが繰延税金資産・負債を発生させ、永久差異は発生させない。地方債利子$5,000は永久差異のため繰延税金なし。税務上の減価償却費が帳簿上を$25,000上回る一時差異は将来課税として解消されるためDTLを生む：$25,000×40%＝$10,000。長期損失引当金が控除可能額を$10,000上回る一時差異は将来控除としてDTAを生む：$10,000×40%＝$4,000。純DTL＝$10,000－$4,000＝$6,000。
